_meta.note: Generated by ops/scripts/dump-node-catalog.sh. Outcome names live in am-node-outcomes.md (hand-curated; some are data-dependent and cant come from REST).

ADDecisionNode.name: AD Decision
ADDecisionNode.help: Verifies that the provided username and password values exist in a specified Active Directory store, and whether they are expired or locked out.

mtlsSecretLabel.title: mTLS Secret Label Identifier
mtlsSecretLabel.description:
Identifier used to create a secret label for mapping to the mTLS certificate in the secret store.
AM uses this label to create a specific secret label for this node. The secret label takes the form am.authentication.nodes.ad.decision.mtls.{{identifier}}.cert where {{identifier}} is the value of mTLS Secret Label Identifier. The label can only contain characters {{a-z}} {{A-Z}} {{0-9}} {{.}} and cannot start or end with {{.}}.

userProfileAttribute.title: Attribute Used to Retrieve User Profile
userProfileAttribute.description:
Specifies the attribute used to retrieve the profile of a user from the directory server.

The user search will have already happened, as specified by the Attributes Used to Search for a User to be Authenticated and User Search Filter properties.

heartbeatTimeUnit.title: LDAP Connection Heartbeat Time Unit
heartbeatTimeUnit.description:
Specifies the time unit corresponding to LDAP Connection Heartbeat Interval.

Default: Seconds

adminDn.title: Bind User DN
adminDn.description:
Specify the user DN used to bind to the LDAP user data store.

Note: Do not use cn=Directory Manager in production systems.If mTLS is enabled, this attribute is ignored.

ldapConnectionMode.title: LDAP Connection Mode
ldapConnectionMode.description:
Specifies whether to use SSL or StartTLS to connect to the LDAP user data store.

AM must be able to trust the certificates used.

accountSearchBaseDn.title: DN to Start User Search
accountSearchBaseDn.description:
Specify the DN from which to start the user search.

More specific DNs, such as ou=sales,dc=example,dc=com, result in better search performance.If multiple entries exist in the store with identical attribute values, ensure this property is specific enough to return only one entry.

minimumPasswordLength.title: Minimum Password Length
minimumPasswordLength.description: Specifies the minimum acceptable password length.

returnAccountLockedMessage.title: Return Account Locked Message
returnAccountLockedMessage.description: When disabled the node will return a generic authentication failure message when the user account is locked. When enabled the node will return a specific account locked message whether the correct password was provided or not.

mtlsEnabled.title: mTLS Enabled
mtlsEnabled.description:
Enables mTLS (mutual TLS) between AM and this store. When mTLS is enabled:
  • Set connection mode to LDAPS.
  • The values for Bind User DN and Bind User Password are ignored.
  • You must provide an mTLS Secret Label Identifier.
Instructions for setting up certificates and keystore mappings are in the product documentation.

ldapOperationsTimeout.title: LDAP Operations Timeout
ldapOperationsTimeout.description:
Defines the timeout in milliseconds that ${am.abbr} should wait for a response from the directory server.

Default: 0 (No timeout).

adminPassword.title: Bind User Password
adminPassword.description: Specify the password of the account used to bind to the LDAP user data store.If mTLS is enabled, this attribute is ignored.

userCreationAttrs.title: User Creation Attributes
userCreationAttrs.description:
This list lets you map (external) attribute names from the LDAP directory server to (internal) attribute names used by AM.

The format of this property is:
local attr1|external attr1

primaryServers.title: Primary LDAP Server
primaryServers.description:
Specify one or more primary directory servers.

Specify each directory server in the following format:
host:port

For example, directory_services.example.com:389.

searchScope.title: Search Scope
searchScope.description:
Specifies the extent of searching for users in the directory server.

Scope OBJECT means search only the entry specified as the DN to Start User Search, whereas ONELEVEL means search only the entries that are directly children of that object. SUBTREE means search the entry specified and every entry under it.

returnUserDn.title: Return User DN to DataStore
returnUserDn.description: When enabled, the node returns the DN rather than the User ID.

searchFilterAttributes.title: Attributes Used to Search for a User to be Authenticated
searchFilterAttributes.description:
Specifies the attributes used to match an entry in the directory server to the credentials provided by the user.

The default value of uid will form the following search filter of uid=user. Specifying multiple values such as uid and cn causes the node to create a search filter of (|(uid=user)(cn=user)).

Multiple attribute values allow the user to authenticate with any one of the values. For example, if you have both uid and mail, then Barbara Jensen can authenticate with either bjensen or bjensen@example.com.

affinityLevel.title: LDAP Affinity Level
affinityLevel.description: Level of affinity used to balance requests across LDAP servers. The options are: no affinity, affinity for BIND requests only, affinity for all requests.

userSearchFilter.title: User Search Filter
userSearchFilter.description:
Specifies an additional filter to append to user searches.

For example, searching for mail and specifying a User Search Filter of (objectClass=inetOrgPerson), causes AM to use (&(mail=address)(objectClass=inetOrgPerson)) as the resulting search filter, where address is the mail address provided by the user.

heartbeatInterval.title: LDAP Connection Heartbeat Interval
heartbeatInterval.description:
Specifies how often AM should send a heartbeat request to the directory server to ensure that the connection does not remain idle.

Some network administrators configure firewalls and load balancers to drop connections that are idle for too long. You can turn this off by setting the value to 0 or to a negative number. Set the units for the interval in the LDAP Connection Heartbeat Time Unit property.

mixedCaseForPasswordChangeMessages.title: Use mixed case for password change messages
mixedCaseForPasswordChangeMessages.description: Defines whether password change messages returned are in mixed (sentence) case or uppercase. Default: false

trustAllServerCertificates.title: Trust All Server Certificates
trustAllServerCertificates.description:
When enabled, blindly trust server certificates, including self-signed test certificates.

Note: Use this feature with care as it bypasses the normal certificate verification process.

secondaryServers.title: Secondary LDAP Server
secondaryServers.description:
Specify one or more secondary directory servers.

Specify each directory server in the following format:
host:port

Secondary servers are used when none of the primary servers are available.

For example, directory_services_backup.example.com.

AccountActiveDecisionNode.name: Account Active Decision
AccountActiveDecisionNode.help: Checks if the current user's account is active.

AccountLockoutNode.name: Account Lockout
AccountLockoutNode.help: Locks or unlocks the authenticating user's account profile.

lockAction.title: Lock Action
lockAction.description: If the action is set to LOCK, the node will lock the account.

AgentDataStoreDecisionNode.name: Agent Data Store Decision
AgentDataStoreDecisionNode.help: Verifies that a provided agent ID and password match a web agent or Java agent profile.

authorizedKeys.title: Authorized Keys
authorizedKeys.description: The location of the authorized_keys file (which has the same format as an OpenSSH authorized_keys file) to use to validate remote Amster connections.

AnonymousSessionUpgradeNode.name: Anonymous Session Upgrade
AnonymousSessionUpgradeNode.help: Enables anonymous sessions to be upgraded.

AnonymousUserNode.name: Anonymous User Mapping
AnonymousUserNode.help: Allows users to log in to your application or web site without providing credentials by assuming the identity of a specified, existing user account.

anonymousUserName.title: Anonymous User Name
anonymousUserName.description: The username of the user that will represent the anonymous user. This user account must already exist in the realm.

AppPolicyDecisionNode.name: App Policy Decision
AppPolicyDecisionNode.help: Node that evaluates app policies for the subject identified in the journey.

AuthLevelDecisionNode.name: Auth Level Decision
AuthLevelDecisionNode.help: Compares the current authentication level value against a configured value.

authLevelRequirement.title: Sufficient Authentication Level
authLevelRequirement.description: The current authentication level must be greater than or equal to this value for the decision to return true.

ChoiceCollectorNode.name: Choice Collector
ChoiceCollectorNode.help: Presents the user with two or more options defined within the node's configuration.

choices.title: Choices
choices.description: List of values that represents the choices for the user.

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: Prompt displayed on the choice page.

CombinedMultiFactorRegistrationNode.name: Combined MFA Registration
CombinedMultiFactorRegistrationNode.help: Allows users of mobile devices to register for both Push and OATH authentication methods.

minSharedSecretLength.title: Minimum Secret Key Length
minSharedSecretLength.description: Minimum number of hexadecimal characters allowed for the Secret Key.

generateRecoveryCodes.title: Generate Recovery Codes
generateRecoveryCodes.description: If enabled, the success outcome's transient state will contain a set of recovery codes. If this success outcome is passed into a Recovery Code Display Node, these codes will be presented to the user. A user may use recovery codes to bypass the Push authentication node in the event they have lost their authenticator.

passwordLength.title: One Time Password Length
passwordLength.description: The length of the generated OTP in digits, must be at least 6 and compatible with the hardware/software OTP generators you expect your end-users to use. For example, Google and ForgeRock authenticators support values of 6 and 8.

addChecksum.title: HOTP Checksum Digit
addChecksum.description: This adds a digit to the end of the OTP generated to be used as a checksum to verify the OTP was generated correctly. This is in addition to the actual password length. You should only set this if your device supports it.

timeout.title: Registration Response Timeout
timeout.description: The period of time (in seconds) to wait for a response to the registration QR code. If no response is received during this time the QR code times out and the registration process fails.

totpHashAlgorithm.title: TOTP Hash Algorithm
totpHashAlgorithm.description: The Hmac hash algorithm to be used on generating the OTP codes.

bgColor.title: Background Color
bgColor.description: The background color in hex notation to display behind the issuer's logo within the ForgeRock Authenticator app.

truncationOffset.title: HOTP Truncation Offset
truncationOffset.description: This is an option used by the HOTP algorithm that not all devices support. This should be left default unless you know your device uses an offset.

issuer.title: Issuer
issuer.description: A value that appears as an identifier on the user's device. Common choices are a company name, a website, or an AM realm.

policiesJson.title: JSON Authenticator Policies
policiesJson.description: The Authenticator Policies in a JSON format containing the rules to be applied to Authenticator app. For more details consult the SDK documentation. Example: {"biometricAvailable": { },"deviceTampering": {"score": 0.8}}

imgUrl.title: Logo Image URL
imgUrl.description: The location of an image to download and display as the issuer's logo within the ForgeRock Authenticator app.

totpTimeInterval.title: TOTP Time Step Interval
totpTimeInterval.description: This is the time interval that one OTP is valid for. For example, if the time step is 30 seconds, then a new OTP will be generated every 30 seconds. This makes a single OTP valid for only 30 seconds.

accountName.title: Account Name
accountName.description: This field allows selection of the user attribute to be used as the user's Account Name. It is used when the user's Push account is stored in the device. If left blank or the selected attribute is empty on user's profile, the account name will be set to the user's username.

scanQRCodeMessage.title: QR code message
scanQRCodeMessage.description: The message with instructions to scan the QR code for registering the device.

CookiePresenceDecisionNode.name: Cookie Presence Decision Node
CookiePresenceDecisionNode.help: Checks if a named cookie is present in the incoming authentication request.

DataStoreDecisionNode.name: Data Store Decision
DataStoreDecisionNode.help: Verifies that the username and password values exist in the data store configured in the realm.

DebugNode.name: Debug Node
DebugNode.help: Activate debug mode to output tree data to debug logs. Optionally, enable the debug popup to display debug logs as you traverse through the login tree

popupEnabled.title: Enable Debug Popup
popupEnabled.description: If enabled, a popup window will display debug logs as you step through the login tree

title.title: Title
title.description: The title to be displayed on the biometric prompt.

applicationIds.title: Application Ids
applicationIds.description: The Android package name or iOS bundle id that issue the binding request.

timeout.title: Timeout
timeout.description: The number of seconds to wait for a valid device to be bound before failing.

skewAllowance.title: Skew Allowance
skewAllowance.description: Defines the maximum allowed clock difference in seconds between the end-user device and PingAM.

postponeDeviceProfileStorage.title: Store Device Data in Transient State
postponeDeviceProfileStorage.description: If enabled, the device is not stored directly in the user profile when the node completes successfully. Instead, the device information is placed into the transient state for use by subsequent nodes using the key 'DeviceBinding.DEVICE'. The provided 'Device Binding Storage Node' can be used for this purpose.

maxSavedDevices.title: Maximum Saved Devices
maxSavedDevices.description: Specify the maximum number of devices to save in a user's profile. 0 for unlimited.

authenticationType.title: Authentication Type
authenticationType.description: Select the authentication type for accessing the Keys in the device.

subtitle.title: Subtitle
subtitle.description: The subtitle to be displayed on the biometric prompt.

attestation.title: Android Key Attestation
attestation.description: Use the Android hardware key attestation service to verify that the certificates used for device binding are stored securely in the device's hardware-backed keystore. For more information, refer to Verifying hardware-backed key pairs with Key Attestation | Android Developers.

description.title: Description
description.description: The description to be displayed on the biometric prompt.

locations.title: Trusted Locations
locations.description: Specify the latitude and longitude of trusted locations. Separate the values with a comma; for example, `-123.177201,49.164532`.

distance.title: Geofence Radius (km)
distance.description: Specifies the maximum distance, in kilometers, that a device can be from the specified trusted location(s).

distance.title: Maximum Radius (km)
distance.description: Specifies the maximum distance, in kilometers, that a device can be from a previously saved location.

acceptableVariance.title: Acceptable Variance
acceptableVariance.description: Specify the maximum amount of device attribute differences that is still acceptable for a match.

expiration.title: Expiration
expiration.description: Specify the number of days until profile expiration. Expired profiles will not match.

script.title: Custom Matching Script

enum_titles: Device Profile Match Template - Decision Node Script
Next Generation Device Match Node Script

enumNames: Device Profile Match Template - Decision Node Script
Next Generation Device Match Node Script

useScript.title: Use Custom Matching Script
useScript.description: Use a custom script for matching device profiles. When enabled, the Acceptable Variance and Expiration properties are ignored. The script's type has to be: Decision Node Script.

deviceLocation.title: Collect Device Location
deviceLocation.description: Instructs the client to collect device location.

maximumSize.title: Maximum Profile Size (KB)
maximumSize.description: Specify the maximum accepted size for a device profile in kilobytes. If the returned profile size exceeds this maximum the authentication will fail. Note that larger profiles can impact performance.

deviceMetadata.title: Collect Device Metadata
deviceMetadata.description: Instructs the client to collect device metadata.

message.title: Message
message.description: Optional message to display to the user when capturing device information. Enter a locale in the KEY field, for example `en-us`, and the localized message as the VALUE.

saveDeviceMetadata.title: Save Device Metadata
saveDeviceMetadata.description: Specify whether device metadata should be saved.

saveDeviceLocation.title: Save Device Location
saveDeviceLocation.description: Specify whether device location should be saved.

variableName.title: Device Name Variable
variableName.description: Specify an existing variable name, in shared state, that contains the value to use as this device's name.

maxSavedProfiles.title: Maximum Saved Profiles
maxSavedProfiles.description: Specify the maximum number of device profiles to save in a user's profile. When the maximum is reached, saving a new profile replaces the least-recently used profile.

description.title: Description
description.description: The description to be displayed on the biometric prompt.

timeout.title: Timeout
timeout.description: The number of seconds to wait for a valid device to be bound before failing.

sharedStateAttribute.title: Shared State Attribute As Challenge.
sharedStateAttribute.description: Shared State attribute as challenge for signing.

subtitle.title: Subtitle
subtitle.description: The subtitle to be displayed on the biometric prompt.

title.title: Title
title.description: The title to be displayed on the biometric prompt.

applicationIds.title: Application Ids
applicationIds.description: The Android package names or iOS bundle IDs that issue the binding request.

captureFailure.title: Capture Failure
captureFailure.description:
If enabled, instead of stopping the journey's execution for error scenario, it will route to the Failure outcome, failure reason is captured in shared state, accessible to subsequent nodes in the journey with variable DeviceSigningVerifierNode.FAILURE:
  • INVALID_CLAIM - Failed to validate the token claim attribute.
  • INVALID_SIGNATURE - Failed to validate the token signature.
  • INVALID_USER - Account does not exist.
  • NOT_ACTIVE_USER - Account is not active or locked out.
  • INVALID_SUBJECT - Failed to validate the token subject.

skewAllowance.title: Skew Allowance
skewAllowance.description: Defines the maximum allowed clock difference in seconds between the end-user device and PingAM.

score.title: Score Threshold
score.description: Specifies the score threshold for determining if a device is jailbroken or rooted. Jailbreak scores received from the client will be between 0 and 1. The higher the score, the more likely the device is jailbroken (Emulators will score a 1).

EnableDeviceManagementNode.name: Enable Device Management
EnableDeviceManagementNode.help: Lets the user manage their devices in a self-service journey.

deviceEnforcementStrategy.title: Device Check Enforcement Strategy
deviceEnforcementStrategy.description: The method AM uses to verify the user's device. Options are: SAME: The user must authenticate with the same device type. ANY: The user can authenticate with any device type (WebAuthn, Push, or OATH). NONE: AM doesn't check the device type.

FlowControlNode.name: Flow Control
FlowControlNode.help: Control the flow of authentication requests.

outcomeBDisplayName.title: Path B Name
outcomeBDisplayName.description: The display name for the outcome of path B.

outcomeADisplayName.title: Path A Name
outcomeADisplayName.description: The display name for the outcome of path A.

percentageA.title: Path A Percentage
percentageA.description: Percentage of requests to send down path A. The remaining percentage will go down path B.

GetAuthenticatorAppNode.name: Get Authenticator App
GetAuthenticatorAppNode.help: Presents a custom, localized message to the user with links to the Authenticator on App stores.

googleLink.title: Google Play URL
googleLink.description: URL of the app to download on the Google Play Store.

appleLink.title: Apple App Store URL
appleLink.description: URL of the app to download on the Apple App Store.

message.title: Get App Authenticator Message
message.description: Localized message providing the authenticator app links. The keys {{appleLink}} and {{googleLink}} are used as wildcards. The default message is: Get the app from the {{appleLink}} or on {{googleLink}}. You can also use {{appleLabel}} and {{googleLabel}} to customize the message including HTML elements, e.g.: Apple: {{appleLabel}}

IdentityAssertionDecisionNode.name: Identity Assertion
IdentityAssertionDecisionNode.help: This node redirects the user to an Identity Assertion server, and receives the redirect back. It ensures the communication with the server is secure.

serverToSharedStateMap.title: Mapping from server result (optional)
serverToSharedStateMap.description: Map the Identity Assertion server result JWT claims to fields in the shared node state. If the server requires no data, you don't need to add mappings here. The key is the server assertion JWT claim containing the data, and the value is the shared node state key to map to. By default, the JWT 'principal' claim is mapped to the AM shared state 'username'.

route.title: Route
route.description: The Identity Assertion route/endpoint that will handle assertion requests.

serverId.title: Identity Assertion server ID
serverId.description: ID of the Identity Assertion server that will handle assertion requests (composed of ID and realm).

sharedStateToServerMap.title: Mapping to server claims (optional)
sharedStateToServerMap.description: Map fields in the shared node state to Identity Assertion server request JWT claims. If the server requires no data, you don't need to add mappings here. The key is the shared node state key, and the value is the JWT claim to map to.

IotAuthenticationNode.name: Authenticate Thing
IotAuthenticationNode.help: Authenticate a thing using a signed JWT. Authentication is successful if an identity for the thing exists and the JWT can be successfully verified in accordance with the chosen JWT Authentication Method. Registration is required if no identity exists for the thing.

jwtAuthenticationMethod.title: JWT Authentication Method
jwtAuthenticationMethod.description:
Choose the required JWT authentication method.

Proof of Possession: Prove that the signer of the JWT is the owner of the key by including a challenge nonce in the JWT. Validation will be done in accordance with the JWT Proof of Possession specification.

Client Assertion: Present a JWT Bearer token for authentication. Validation will be done in accordance with the OAuth 2.0 JWT Profile for Client Authentication.

issueRestrictedToken.title: Issue Restricted Token
issueRestrictedToken.description: The session token issued on successful authentication will be modified by adding a proof of possession restriction to it. Any requests accompanied by the token must be signed with the key that was used to sign the authentication JWT.

additionalAudienceValues.title: Additional Audience Values
additionalAudienceValues.description: The additional audience values that will be permitted when verifying JWTs. These audience values will be in addition to the AM base, issuer and token endpoint URIs for the Client Assertion authentication method or the realm path for Proof of Possession.

IotRegistrationNode.name: Register Thing
IotRegistrationNode.help: Register a thing using a signed JWT. Registration is successful if the JWT can be successfully verified in accordance with the chosen JWT Registration Method. The JWT claims are used to create a new identity for a thing.

jwtRegistrationMethod.title: JWT Registration Method
jwtRegistrationMethod.description:
Choose the required JWT registration method.

Proof of Possession & Certificate: Register using a Proof of Possession JWT that includes an X.509 certificate for providing trust. A challenge nonce will be presented in the callback that must be included in the signed JWT.

Proof of Possession & Software Statement: Register using a Proof of Possession JWT and a Software Statement for providing trust. A challenge nonce will be presented in the callback that must be included in the signed Proof of Possession JWT. The claims in the Software Statement will take precedence over the claims in the Proof of Possession JWT.

Proof of Possession: Register using a Proof of Possession JWT without using a trusted third party. A challenge nonce will be presented in the callback that must be included in the signed JWT.

Software Statement: Register using a Software Statement without doing proof of possession. If this registration method is chosen then the resultant session token will not include a proof of possession restriction.

verifySubject.title: Verify Certificate Subject
verifySubject.description: Verify that the subject provided in the JWT is the same as either the X.509 certificate subject CN or UID. Only applies when "Proof of Possession & Certificate" JWT Registration Method is selected.

allowAttributeOverwrite.title: Overwrite Attributes
allowAttributeOverwrite.description: Allow existing identity attributes to be overwritten when new claims are provided for the thing.

createIdentity.title: Create Identity
createIdentity.description: Create a new identity for the thing if one does not exist already.

claimToAttributeMapping.title: Claim to Attribute Mapping
claimToAttributeMapping.description: Map the verified claims to the thing's attributes. The key is the name of the claim in the JWT. The value is the name of the attribute in the data store.

defaultAttributeValues.title: Default Attribute Values
defaultAttributeValues.description: Provide default values for the thing's attributes. The key is the name of the attribute in the data store. The value is the value of the attribute.

allowKeyRotation.title: Rotate Confirmation Key
allowKeyRotation.description: Allow multiple confirmation keys to be registered for a thing.

JwtPasswordReplayNode.name: JWT Password Replay
JwtPasswordReplayNode.help: Node supporting password capture and replay to facilitate SSO in legacy applications. The user password is captured on the session in an encrypted JWT, for the given session property name. Clients must be configured to be able to decrypt the JWT to replay the password.

encryptionKeySecretLabelIdentifier.title: Encryption Key Secret Label Identifier
encryptionKeySecretLabelIdentifier.description: Identifier used to create a secret label for mapping to a secret in a secret store. AM uses this identifier to create a specific secret label for this node. The secret label takes the form am.authentication.nodes.jwt.replay.{{identifier}}.encryption where {{identifier}} is the value of the Encryption Key Secret Label Identifier. The identifier can only contain characters {{a-z}} {{A-Z}} {{0-9}} {{.}} and cannot start or end with {{.}}.

jwtSessionProperty.title: JWT Session Property Name
jwtSessionProperty.description: The name of the session property where the JWT will be stored at the end of the journey. The default value is sunIdentityUserPassword which is recognized by PingGateway.

LdapDecisionNode.name: LDAP Decision
LdapDecisionNode.help: Verifies that the provided username and password values exist in a specified LDAP user data store, and whether they are expired or locked out.

beheraEnabled.title: LDAP Behera Password Policy Support
beheraEnabled.description:
Enables support for modern LDAP password policies.

LDAP Behera Password policies are supported by modern LDAP servers such as DS. If this functionality is disabled then only the older Netscape VCHU password policy standard will be enforced.

ldapConnectionMode.title: LDAP Connection Mode
ldapConnectionMode.description:
Specifies whether to use SSL or StartTLS to connect to the LDAP user data store.

AM must be able to trust the certificates used.

userCreationAttrs.title: User Creation Attributes
userCreationAttrs.description:
This list lets you map (external) attribute names from the LDAP directory server to (internal) attribute names used by AM.

The format of this property is:
local attr1|external attr1

returnUserDn.title: Return User DN to DataStore
returnUserDn.description: When enabled, the node returns the DN rather than the User ID.

MessageNode.name: Message Node
MessageNode.help: Presents a custom, localized message to the user and prompts them to choose a positive or negative answer.

messageYes.title: Positive answer
messageYes.description: Localisation overrides - as key fill shortcut for language (first will be used as default if not empty or "Yes" if empty), value is positive answer for language defined by key.

messageNo.title: Negative answer
messageNo.description: Localisation overrides - as key fill shortcut for language (first will be used as default if not empty or "No" if empty), value is negative answer for language defined by key.

message.title: Message
message.description: Localisation overrides - as key fill shortcut for language (first will be used as default if not empty or "Default message" if empty), value is message for language defined by key.

MetadataNode.name: State metadata
MetadataNode.help: Returns selected attributes from the shared state as metadata

attributes.title: Attributes
attributes.description: List of attributes that will be selected from the shared state (if they exist) and will be returned to the user-agent in a metadata callback.

MeterNode.name: Meter
MeterNode.help: Increments a specified metric key each time tree evaluation passes through the node.

metricKey.title: Metric Key
metricKey.description: Identifier of metric to update when this node is processed.

MultiFactorRegistrationOptionsNode.name: MFA Registration Options
MultiFactorRegistrationOptionsNode.help: Presents a custom, localized message to the user and prompts the user to choose to register, skip or opt-out the second factor authentication method

skipStepLabel.title: Skip this Step
skipStepLabel.description: Localisation for the Skip this Step button.

getApp.title: Display Get Authenticator App
getApp.description: If enabled, display the 'Get the App' button

mandatory.title: Remove 'skip' option
mandatory.description: If checked, users will no longer be able to skip the module, and must interact with it.

message.title: Message
message.description: Localised message for skip the second factor authentication

getAppLabel.title: Get Authenticator App
getAppLabel.description: Localisation for the Get Authenticator App button.

registerDeviceLabel.title: Register Device
registerDeviceLabel.description: Localisation for the Register Device button.

OathDeviceStorageNode.name: OATH Device Storage Node
OathDeviceStorageNode.help: Stores devices which have previously been saved into the transient state by the OathRegistrationNode.

OathRegistrationNode.name: OATH Registration
OathRegistrationNode.help: Allows users of mobile devices to register One-Time Password for use during authentication.

generateRecoveryCodes.title: Generate Recovery Codes
generateRecoveryCodes.description: If enabled, the success outcome's transient state will contain a set of recovery codes. If this success outcome is passed into a Recovery Code Display Node, these codes will be presented to the user. A user may use recovery codes to bypass the Push authentication node in the event they have lost their authenticator.

totpTimeInterval.title: TOTP Time Step Interval
totpTimeInterval.description: This is the time interval that one OTP is valid for. For example, if the time step is 30 seconds, then a new OTP will be generated every 30 seconds. This makes a single OTP valid for only 30 seconds.

minSharedSecretLength.title: Minimum Secret Key Length
minSharedSecretLength.description: Number of hexadecimal characters allowed for the Secret Key.

postponeDeviceProfileStorage.title: Store device data in shared state
postponeDeviceProfileStorage.description: If enabled, the device data isn't stored directly in the user's profile on successful completion of the node. Instead, the node adds the device data, as a base64-encoded string, to the 'oathDeviceProfile' property in the shared node state. This string is decoded as an unescaped plain string representation of a JSON object. Use the 'OATH Device Storage Node' to store the device data in the user's profile.

issuer.title: Issuer
issuer.description: A value that appears as an identifier on the user's device. Common choices are a company name, a web site, or an AM realm.

totpHashAlgorithm.title: TOTP Hash Algorithm
totpHashAlgorithm.description: The Hmac hash algorithm to be used on generating the OTP codes.

imgUrl.title: Logo Image URL
imgUrl.description: The location of an image to download and display as the issuer's logo within the ForgeRock Authenticator app.

scanQRCodeMessage.title: QR code message
scanQRCodeMessage.description: The message with instructions to scan the QR code for registering the device.

addChecksum.title: HOTP Checksum Digit
addChecksum.description: This adds a digit to the end of the OTP generated to be used as a checksum to verify the OTP was generated correctly. This is in addition to the actual password length. You should only set this if your device supports it.

accountName.title: Account Name
accountName.description: This field allows selection of the user attribute to be used as the display name for user's Account. It is used when the user's OATH account is stored in the device. If left blank or the selected attribute is empty on the user's profile, the account name will be set to the user's username.

truncationOffset.title: HOTP Truncation Offset
truncationOffset.description: This is an option used by the HOTP algorithm that not all devices support. This should be left default unless you know your device uses an offset.

bgColor.title: Background Color
bgColor.description: The background color in hex notation to display behind the issuer's logo within the ForgeRock Authenticator app.

passwordLength.title: One Time Password Length
passwordLength.description: The length of the generated OTP in digits, must be at least 6 and compatible with the hardware/software OTP generators you expect your end-users to use. For example, Google and ForgeRock authenticators support values of 6 and 8.

OathTokenVerifierNode.name: OATH Token Verifier
OathTokenVerifierNode.help: Collects and verify the One-Time Password code provided by the user.

isRecoveryCodeAllowed.title: Allow recovery codes
isRecoveryCodeAllowed.description: Allow users to use one of the recovery codes to proceed with the login.

totpTimeInterval.title: TOTP Time Step Interval
totpTimeInterval.description: This is the time interval that one OTP is valid for. For example, if the time step is 30 seconds, then a new OTP will be generated every 30 seconds. This makes a single OTP valid for only 30 seconds.

totpHashAlgorithm.title: TOTP Hash Algorithm
totpHashAlgorithm.description: The Hmac hash algorithm to be used on generating the OTP codes.

maximumAllowedClockDrift.title: TOTP Maximum Allowed Clock Drift
maximumAllowedClockDrift.description: Number of time steps a client is allowed to get out of sync with the server before manual resynchronization is required. For example, with 3 allowed drifts and a time step interval of 30 seconds the server will allow codes from up to 90 seconds from the current time to be treated as the current time step. The drift for a user's device is calculated each time they enter a new code. If the drift exceeds this value, the user's authentication code will be rejected.

hotpWindowSize.title: HOTP Window Size
hotpWindowSize.description: This sets the window that the OTP device and the server counter can be out of sync. For example, if the window size is 100 and the servers last successful login was at counter value 2, then the server will accept a OTP from the OTP device that is from device counter 3 to 102.

totpTimeSteps.title: TOTP Time Steps
totpTimeSteps.description: This is the number of time step intervals to check the received OTP against both forward in time and back in time. For example, with 1 time step and a time step interval of 30 seconds the server will allow a code between the previous code, the current code and the next code.

OidcNode.name: OIDC ID Token Validator
OidcNode.help: Validates an OIDC provider's ID Token and transforms the attributes according to a configured script.

headerName.title: ID Token Header Name
headerName.description: Name of header referencing the ID Token.

unreasonableLifetimeLimit.title: Unreasonable Lifetime Limit
unreasonableLifetimeLimit.description: During token validation AM enforces that the token must expire within the specified duration and if the "iat" claim value is present, the token must not be older than the specified duration. This value should be in minutes.

idTokenIssuer.title: Token Issuer
idTokenIssuer.description: Name of the OpenID Connect ID token issuer. Value must match the iss field in issued ID Token e.g. accounts.google.com

oidcValidationValue.title: OpenID Connect Validation Value
oidcValidationValue.description:
Depending on the value of the OpenID Connect Validation Type property, this property holds the full URL to the discovery or JWK location. If OpenID Connect Validation Type is set to Client Secret, AM ignores this property and uses the value of the Client Secret.

Example: https://accounts.google.com/.well-known/openid-configuration

secretId.title: Client Secret Label
secretId.description:
A secret label for mapping the OIDC Client Secret to a secret in the secret store.
The label can only contain characters {{a-z}} {{A-Z}} {{0-9}} {{.}} and cannot start or end with {{.}}.

audienceName.title: Audience name
audienceName.description: The audience name for this OpenID Connect node. This will be used to check that the ID token received is intended for this node as an audience.

inputs.title: Script Inputs
inputs.description: A list of state inputs that can be used by the script.

authorisedParties.title: Authorized parties
authorisedParties.description: A list of case-sensitive accepted authorized parties which can be either string or URI values. This will be checked against the authorized party claim of the ID token.

oidcValidationType.title: OpenID Connect Validation Type
oidcValidationType.description:
In order to validate the ID token from the OpenID Connect provider, the node needs either a URL to get the public keys for the provider, or the symmetric key for an ID token signed with a HMAC-based algorithm.

By default, the configuration type is .well-known/openid-configuration_url. This means the node should retrieve the keys based on information in the OpenID Connect Provider Configuration Document.

You can instead configure the authentication node to validate the ID token signature with the client secret key you provide, or to validate the ID token with the keys retrieved from the URL to the OpenID Connect provider's JSON web key set.

script.title: Transformation Script
script.description: A script that can transform the ID token's claims into object data.

enum_titles: Social Identity Provider Profile Transformation Script

enumNames: Social Identity Provider Profile Transformation Script

OneTimePasswordCollectorDecisionNode.name: OTP Collector Decision
OneTimePasswordCollectorDecisionNode.help: Prompts the user to enter their one-time password and verifies that input.

passwordExpiryTime.title: One Time Password Validity Length
passwordExpiryTime.description: This One Time Password will remain valid for this period in minutes.

OneTimePasswordGeneratorNode.name: HOTP Generator
OneTimePasswordGeneratorNode.help: Creates a string of random digits, of the length specified, to be used in one-time password verification.

length.title: One Time Password Length
length.description: The length in characters of the one time password.

OneTimePasswordSmsSenderNode.name: OTP SMS Sender
OneTimePasswordSmsSenderNode.help: Uses an email-to-SMS gateway provider to send an SMS message containing a generated one-time password to the user.

sslOption.title: Mail Server Secure Connection
sslOption.description: This setting controls whether the authentication module communicates with the mail server using SSL/TLS.

password.title: Mail Server Authentication Password
password.description: The password to use when the mail server uses SMTP authentication. This property is deprecated. Use the Mail Server Secret Label Identifier instead. The password is  ignored if you set a Mail Server Secret Label Identifier.

username.title: Mail Server Authentication Username
username.description: The username to use when the mail server is using SMTP authentication.

smsContent.title: The content of the message
smsContent.description: This is the content of the message that will be sent. All occurrence of {{OTP}} will be replaced with the One Time Password.

fromEmailAddress.title: Email From Address
fromEmailAddress.description: Emails from the OTP Email Sender node will come from this address.

secretLabelIdentifier.title: Mail Server Secret Label Identifier
secretLabelIdentifier.description:
Label identifier used to create a secret label for mapping to the secret in the secret store.
AM uses this label identifier to create a specific secret label for this node. The secret label takes the form am.authentication.nodes.otp.sms.{{identifier}}.password where {{identifier}} is the value of Mail Server Secret Label Identifier. The identifier can only contain characters {{a-z}} {{A-Z}} {{0-9}} {{.}} and cannot start or end with {{.}}. If you set a Mail Server Secret Label Identifier and AM finds a matching secret in a secret store, the Mail Server Authentication Password is ignored.

mobilePhoneAttributeName.title: Mobile Phone Number Attribute Name
mobilePhoneAttributeName.description: This is the attribute name used for a requested text message.

smsSubject.title: The subject of the message
smsSubject.description: This is the subject of the message that will be sent.

hostName.title: Mail Server Host Name
hostName.description: The name of the mail server OpenAM will use to send the messages.

smsGatewayImplementationClass.title: Gateway Implementation Class
smsGatewayImplementationClass.description:
The OTP SMS Sender node uses this class to send SMS messages.

The gateway class must implement the following interface:
com.sun.identity.authentication.modules.hotp.SMSGateway

mobileCarrierAttributeName.title: Mobile Carrier Attribute Name
mobileCarrierAttributeName.description: This is the attribute name used for a mobile carrier domain for sending SMS messages.

hostPort.title: Mail Server Host Port
hostPort.description: The port of the mail server. The default port for SMTP is 25, if using SSL the default port is 465.

OneTimePasswordSmtpSenderNode.name: OTP Email Sender
OneTimePasswordSmtpSenderNode.help: Sends an email containing a generated one-time password to the user.

password.title: Mail Server Authentication Password
password.description: The password to use when the mail server uses SMTP authentication. This property is deprecated. Use the Mail Server Secret Label Identifier instead. The password is  ignored if you set a Mail Server Secret Label Identifier.

username.title: Mail Server Authentication Username
username.description: The username to use when the mail server is using SMTP authentication.

emailAttribute.title: Email Attribute Name
emailAttribute.description: This is the attribute name used by the OTP Sender to email the user.

hostName.title: Mail Server Host Name
hostName.description: The name of the mail server OpenAM will use to send the messages.

emailSubject.title: The subject of the email
emailSubject.description: This is the subject of the email that will be sent.

hostPort.title: Mail Server Host Port
hostPort.description: The port of the mail server. The default port for SMTP is 25, if using SSL the default port is 465.

sslOption.title: Mail Server Secure Connection
sslOption.description: This setting controls whether the authentication module communicates with the mail server using SSL/TLS.

secretLabelIdentifier.title: Mail Server Secret Label Identifier
secretLabelIdentifier.description:
Label identifier used to create a secret label for mapping to the secret in the secret store.
AM uses this label identifier to create a specific secret label for this node. The secret label takes the form am.authentication.nodes.otp.mail.{{identifier}}.password where {{identifier}} is the value of Mail Server Secret Label Identifier. The identifier can only contain characters {{a-z}} {{A-Z}} {{0-9}} {{.}} and cannot start or end with {{.}}. If you set a Mail Server Secret Label Identifier and AM finds a matching secret in a secret store, the Mail Server Authentication Password is ignored.

smsGatewayImplementationClass.title: Gateway Implementation Class
smsGatewayImplementationClass.description:
The OTP Email Sender node uses this class to send email messages.

The gateway class must implement the following interface:
com.sun.identity.authentication.modules.hotp.SMSGateway

emailContent.title: The content of the email
emailContent.description: This is the content of the email that will be sent. All occurrence of {{OTP}} will be replaced with the One Time Password.

fromEmailAddress.title: Email From Address
fromEmailAddress.description: Emails from the OTP Email Sender node will come from this address.

OptOutMultiFactorAuthenticationNode.name: Opt-out Multi-Factor Authentication
OptOutMultiFactorAuthenticationNode.help: Set skippable attribute on the authenticating user's account profile.

PasswordCollectorNode.name: Password Collector
PasswordCollectorNode.help: Prompts the user to enter their password.

PersistentCookieDecisionNode.name: Persistent Cookie Decision
PersistentCookieDecisionNode.help: Checks for the existence of the persistent cookie specified in the Persistent cookie name property.

hmacSigningKey.title: HMAC Signing Key
hmacSigningKey.description: Base64-encoded 256-bit key to use for HMAC signing of the cookie. This property is deprecated. Use the HMAC Signing Key Secret Label Identifier instead. The signing key is  ignored if you set an HMAC Signing Key Secret Label Identifier.

enforceClientIp.title: Enforce Client IP
enforceClientIp.description: Enforces that the persistent cookie can only be used from the same client IP to which the cookie was issued.

useSecureCookie.title: Use Secure Cookie
useSecureCookie.description: Sets the persistent cookie as "Secure". This will be ignored and set to true if SameSite is set to "NONE".

sameSite.title: Persistent Cookie SameSite attribute
sameSite.description:
Sets the same site attribute of the persistent cookie. This value controls when a browser sends the cookie in cross-site requests. Possible values include:
* `STRICT`: The cookie is only sent when navigating within the same site. It's not sent on any cross-site requests, even for top-level navigations.
* `LAX`: The cookie is sent on same-site requests and top-level navigations. It's not sent on subresource requests (like images or scripts loaded from a third-party site).
* `NONE`: There are no restrictions on the cookie domain.

persistentCookieName.title: Persistent Cookie Name
persistentCookieName.description: The name of the persistent cookie.

idleTimeout.title: Idle Timeout
idleTimeout.description: The maximum idle time between requests before the cookie is invalidated, in hours.

hmacSigningKeySecretLabelIdentifier.title: HMAC Signing Key Secret Label Identifier
hmacSigningKeySecretLabelIdentifier.description:
Identifier used to create a secret label for mapping to a secret in a secret store.
AM uses this identifier to create a specific secret label for this node. The secret label takes the form am.authentication.nodes.persistentcookie.{{identifier}}.signing where {{identifier}} is the value of HMAC Signing Key Secret Label Identifier. The identifier can only contain characters {{a-z}} {{A-Z}} {{0-9}} {{.}} and cannot start or end with {{.}}. If you set an HMAC Signing Key Secret Label Identifier and AM finds a matching secret in a secret store, the HMAC Signing Key is ignored.

PingOneCreateUserNode.name: PingOne Create User
PingOneCreateUserNode.help: This node creates a user on the PingOne platform. If the user is successfully created, the node sets the PingOne user ID in the shared state under a key named pingOneUserId.

captureFailure.title: Capture failure
captureFailure.description: If selected, a failure is captured in shared state under a key named pingOneCreateUserFailureReason for use by subsequent nodes in the journey.

anonymizedUser.title: Anonymized user
anonymizedUser.description: Whether to create the anonymized PingOne user.

amIdentityAttribute.title: AM identity attribute
amIdentityAttribute.description: The attribute of the existing AM identity object that will be used as key to identity the user in the PingOne directory server.

populationId.title: Population ID
populationId.description:
The unique identifier for the PingOne population.

If not specified, the node uses the environment's default population.

pingOneWorker.title: PingOne Worker service ID
pingOneWorker.description: The ID of the PingOne Worker service for connecting to PingOne.

PingOneDeleteUserNode.name: PingOne Delete User
PingOneDeleteUserNode.help:
This node deletes a user on the PingOne platform.

The node gets the PingOne user ID from shared state using the pingOneUserId key.

pingOneWorker.title: PingOne Worker service ID
pingOneWorker.description: The ID of the PingOne Worker service for connecting to PingOne.

captureFailure.title: Capture failure
captureFailure.description: If selected, a failure is captured in shared state under a key named pingOneDeleteUserFailureReason for use by subsequent nodes in the journey.

PingOneIdentityMatchNode.name: PingOne Identity Match
PingOneIdentityMatchNode.help:
This node checks whether the user of the Ping AM exists in the PingOne platform.

If the user already exists in the PingOne platform, the node sets the PingOne user ID in the shared state under a key named pingOneUserId.

amIdentityAttribute.title: AM identity attribute
amIdentityAttribute.description: The attribute of the existing AM identity object that will be used to query the PingOne user. If there are multiple entries with the same attribute value in the PingOne directory server, ensure that this property is specific enough to retrieve only one entry.

captureFailure.title: Capture failure
captureFailure.description: If selected, a failure is captured in shared state under a keynamed pingOneIdentityMatchFailureReason for use by subsequent nodes in the journey.

pingIdentityAttribute.title: PingOne identity attribute
pingIdentityAttribute.description: The PingOne user attribute to be used for matching the AM user. If this attribute identifies more than one user with the same value in the PingOne system, it will fail.

pingOneWorker.title: PingOne Worker service ID
pingOneWorker.description: The ID of the PingOne Worker service for connecting to PingOne.

PingOneVerifyCompletionDecisionNode.name: PingOne Verify Completion Decision
PingOneVerifyCompletionDecisionNode.help:
The PingOne Verify Completion Decision node determines the completion status of the end user's identity verification on the PingOne platform.

The node checks the previous identity verification for the end user and returns an outcome based on the end user's current verification status.

With a custom script, the node can evaluate any of the user's Verify Transactions and their metadata, instead of just the last transaction.

useFilterScript.title: Use a script to process Verify transactions

scriptInputs.title: Script Inputs
scriptInputs.description: A list of inputs required by the configuration script.

captureFailure.title: Capture failure
captureFailure.description: If selected, a failure is captured in shared state under a key named pingOneVerifyCompletionFailureReason for use by subsequent nodes in the journey.

script.title: Manage Verify transactions script
script.description: A custom Decision Node script that evaluates the user's Verify transactions and their metadata.

pingOneWorker.title: PingOne Worker service ID
pingOneWorker.description: The ID of the PingOne Worker service for connecting to PingOne.

PingOneVerifyEvaluationNode.name: PingOne Verify Evaluation
PingOneVerifyEvaluationNode.help: The PingOne Verify Evaluation node lets administrators integrate PingOne Verify for identity verification within a journey.

captureFailure.title: Capture failure
captureFailure.description: If selected, a failure is captured in shared state under a key named pingOneVerifyEvaluationFailureReason for use by subsequent nodes in the journey.

pingOneWorker.title: PingOne Worker service ID
pingOneWorker.description: The ID of the PingOne Worker service for connecting to PingOne.

waitingMessage.title: Waiting message
waitingMessage.description: Localization overrides for the waiting message. This is a map of locale to message.

biographicMatching.title: User profile biographic matching
biographicMatching.description: Attributes from the user profile to compare with the data extracted from the government identity document provided by the client. If empty, no user profile biographic matching verification is performed. Key is the biographic matching requirement. Value is the identity attribute name. Keys must be unique across user profile biographic matching and node state biographic matching. Accepted biographic matching keys: referenceSelfie, phone, email, given_name, family_name, name, address, and birth_date.

autoRedirect.title: Automatic redirect
autoRedirect.description: If enabled, when the delivery method is Redirect, the user is automatically redirected back to the journey after successfully completing the verification process in PingOne Verify.

deliveryMethod.title: Verify URL delivery method
deliveryMethod.description:
The delivery method for the Verify URL. Choose from:
  • QRCODE: Display the URL as a QR code (default)
  • EMAIL: Send an email containing the URL to the email address in the user's profile
  • SMS: Send an SMS containing the URL to the phone number in the user's profile
  • REDIRECT: Redirect the user to the Verify Web app

verifyPolicyId.title: Verify policy ID
verifyPolicyId.description:
The ID of the Verify policy.

If not specified, the node uses the environment's default Verify policy.

storeVerifiedData.title: Store verified data
storeVerifiedData.description:
Store the list of verified data submitted by the user in the shared state under a keynamed pingOneVerifyVerifiedData.

Note: The key is empty if the node is unable to retrieve the verified data from PingOne.

timeout.title: Verify transaction timeout
timeout.description: The period of time (in seconds) to wait for a response to the Verify transaction. If no response is received during this time the node times out and the verification process fails.

redirectMessage.title: Redirect message
redirectMessage.description: The message to display to the user after the identity verification process is complete and the user is redirected back to the Journey. This field should only be used if Automatic redirect is disabled.

allowDeliveryMethodSelection.title: Allow user to choose the URL delivery method
allowDeliveryMethodSelection.description: If enabled, prompt the user to select the URL delivery method. This does not apply if the delivery method is set to Redirect.

storeVerificationMetadata.title: Store verification metadata
storeVerificationMetadata.description:
Store verification metadata from PingOne Verify in the shared state under a keynamed pingOneVerifyMetadata.

Note: The key is empty if the node is unable to retrieve the verification metadata from PingOne.

scanQRCodeMessage.title: QR code message
scanQRCodeMessage.description: The message with instructions to scan the QR code to begin the identity verification process.

deliveryMethodMessage.title: Delivery method message
deliveryMethodMessage.description: The message to display to the user allowing them to choose the delivery method to receive the verify URL (QRCODE, SMS, EMAIL).

nodeStateBiographicMatching.title: Node state biographic matching
nodeStateBiographicMatching.description: Attributes from the node state to compare with the data extracted from the government identity document provided by the client. If empty, no node state biographic matching verification is performed. Key is the biographic matching requirement. Value is the identity attribute name. Keys must be unique across user profile biographic matching and node state biographic matching. Accepted biographic matching keys: referenceSelfie, phone, email, given_name, family_name, name, address, and birth_date.

allowSameDeviceVerification.title: Allow same device verification
allowSameDeviceVerification.description: If selected, the user is presented with an option to continue the identity verification process on their current device. This is only available if the delivery method is set to QRCODE.

PolicyDecisionNode.name: Policy Decision
PolicyDecisionNode.help: Node that evaluates policies for the subject identified in the journey. It doesn't handle advice.

resource.title: Resource
resource.description: The resource name to use for policy evaluation.

policySet.title: Policy Set
policySet.description: The Policy Set (Application) used for policy evaluation.

environment.title: Environment
environment.description: Optional environment parameters to include in the policy evaluation request, specified as key=value pairs.

action.title: Action
action.description: The action name to use for policy evaluation.

PollingWaitNode.name: Polling Wait Node
PollingWaitNode.help: Pauses progress of the authentication tree for a specified number of seconds.

spamDetectionTolerance.title: Spam Tolerance
spamDetectionTolerance.description: How many times a user may resubmit to this node before the authentication is failed.

secondsToWait.title: Seconds To Wait
secondsToWait.description: How many seconds to wait before proceeding to the next node in the tree.

waitingMessage.title: Waiting Message
waitingMessage.description: Localisation overrides for the waiting message. May use {{time}} to get the number of seconds remaining. This is a map of locale to message.

spamDetectionEnabled.title: Enable Spam Detection
spamDetectionEnabled.description: Choose whether spam detection is enforced for this node. Creates a new outcome.

exitable.title: Exitable
exitable.description: Choose whether to display a link which allows the user to leave the node early. Creates a new outcome.

exitMessage.title: Exit Message
exitMessage.description: Localisation overrides for the exit message. The whole string will be displayed as a link. This is a map of locale to message.

PushAuthenticationSenderNode.name: Push Sender
PushAuthenticationSenderNode.help: Sends push notification messages to a device such as a mobile phone, enabling multi-factor authentication.

userMessage.title: User Message
userMessage.description: The message to send to the user. {{user}} and {{issuer}} may be used as replacements for the username and issuer name respectively.

messageTimeout.title: Message Timeout
messageTimeout.description: The duration (in ms) that the message will time out after.

pushType.title: Push Type
pushType.description: This field allows selection of the Push notification type. The 'Tap to Accept' is the default notification type, it just requires the user to Accept it. The 'Display Challenge Code' requires the  user to enter the matching code on the user's device before processing the notification. The 'Use Biometrics to Accept' requires local Biometric authentication to process the notification.

mandatory.title: Remove 'skip' option
mandatory.description: If checked, users will no longer be able to skip the module, and must interact with it.

customPayload.title: Custom Payload Attributes
customPayload.description: Shared state objects which will be included in the message payload sent to the client. Maximum payload for both iOS and Android is 3KB.

contextInfo.title: Share Context Info
contextInfo.description: If checked, additional context information will be added into the notification. The data contains remoteIp, userAgent, and location. Note that the location attribute requires the presence of Device Profile Collector node in the Tree with 'Collect Device Location' config enabled.

captureFailure.title: Capture failure
captureFailure.description: If selected, failure to send the Push Notification is captured in shared state for use by subsequent nodes in the journey.

PushRegistrationNode.name: Push Registration
PushRegistrationNode.help: Allows users of mobile devices to register Push Notifications for use during authentication.

accountName.title: Account Name
accountName.description: This field allows select the user attribute to be used as the user's Account Name. It is used when the user's Push account is   stored in the device. If left blank or the selected attribute is empty on user's profile, the account name will be set to the user's username.

scanQRCodeMessage.title: QR code message
scanQRCodeMessage.description: The message with instructions to scan the QR code for registering the device.

bgColor.title: Background Color
bgColor.description: The background color in hex notation to display behind the issuer's logo within the ForgeRock Authenticator app.

generateRecoveryCodes.title: Generate Recovery Codes
generateRecoveryCodes.description: If enabled, the success outcome's transient state will contain a set of recovery codes. If this success outcome is passed into a Recovery Code Display Node, these codes will be presented to the user. A user may use recovery codes to bypass the Push authentication node in the event they have lost their authenticator.

issuer.title: Issuer
issuer.description: A value that appears as an identifier on the user's device. Common choices are a company name, a web site, or an AM realm.

timeout.title: Registration Response Timeout
timeout.description: The period of time (in seconds) to wait for a response to the registration QR code. If no response is received during this time the QR code times out and the registration process fails.

imgUrl.title: Logo Image URL
imgUrl.description: The location of an image to download and display as the issuer's logo within the ForgeRock Authenticator app.

PushResultVerifierNode.name: Push Result Verifier Node
PushResultVerifierNode.help: Validates the user's response to a previously sent push notification message.

PushWaitNode.name: Push Wait Node
PushWaitNode.help: Pauses progress of the authentication tree for a specified number of seconds while waiting for Push Authentication request being processed. For Push-to-Challenge type, displays the number challenge to be selected by the user.

exitMessage.title: Exit Message
exitMessage.description: Localisation overrides for the exit message. The whole string will be displayed as a link. This is a map of locale to message.

waitingMessage.title: Waiting Message
waitingMessage.description: Localization overrides for the waiting message. May use {{time}} to get the number of seconds remaining. This is a map of locale to message.

challengeMessage.title: Push Challenge Message
challengeMessage.description: Localization overrides for the push challenge message. May use {{challenge}} to get the challenge answer. This is a map of locale to message.

secondsToWait.title: Seconds To Wait
secondsToWait.description: How many seconds to wait before proceeding to the next node in the tree.

QueryParameterNode.name: Query Parameter Node
QueryParameterNode.help: Gets query parameters from the URL of the request and sets them into the sharedState.

queryParametersToBeDelimited.title: Allowed query parameters to be delimited
queryParametersToBeDelimited.description: A list of the "Allowed query parameters" that can take multiple values, and whose values you want to store in the shared state in a comma-delimited list. If you don't delimit the values of a multi-valued query parameter, the node stores the values in the shared state as a single string value.

allowedQueryParameters.title: Allowed query parameters
allowedQueryParameters.description: A list of key-value pairs. KEY is the query parameter name and VALUE is the shared state property name.

RadiusChallengeCollectorNode.name: RADIUS Challenge Collector
RadiusChallengeCollectorNode.help: This node collects the challenge response from the user and stores it in the node state.

RadiusDecisionNode.name: RADIUS Decision
RadiusDecisionNode.help: This node makes a decision based on the result of a RADIUS authentication attempt.

primaryServers.title: Primary RADIUS Servers
primaryServers.description:
A list of primary RADIUS servers that will be used for authentication.

For a single entry, specify the IP address or fully qualified domain name of the RADIUS server.Optionally, append the port number to the server name using a colon, e.g. radius.example.com:1812.

If no port number is specified, the default port number 1812 is used.

Multiple entries allow associations between AM servers and RADIUS servers.

timeout.title: Timeout
timeout.description: Period of time in seconds to wait for the RADIUS server response. This sets the SO_TIMEOUT timeout on the RADIUS packet.

sharedSecretPurpose.title: RADIUS Shared Secret Label Identifier
sharedSecretPurpose.description:
Identifier used to create a secret label for mapping to a secret in a secret store.
AM uses this identifier to create a specific secret label for this node. The secret label takes the form am.authentication.nodes.radius.{{identifier}}.secret where {{identifier}} is the value of RADIUS Shared Secret Label Identifier. The identifier can only contain characters {{a-z}} {{A-Z}} {{0-9}} {{.}} and cannot start or end with {{.}}.

checkForLockout.title: Stop RADIUS binds after lockout
checkForLockout.description: If enabled, no further bind requests are sent to the RADIUS Server when the user is locked out.

secondaryServers.title: Secondary RADIUS Servers
secondaryServers.description:
A list of secondary RADIUS servers that will be used for authentication, in case the primary servers are unavailable.

For a single entry, specify the IP address or fully qualified domain name of the RADIUS server.Optionally, append the port number to the server name using a colon, e.g. radius.example.com:1812.

If no port number is specified, the default port number 1812 is used.

Multiple entries allow associations between AM servers and RADIUS servers.

requireMessageAuthenticator.title: Require Message-Authenticator attribute
requireMessageAuthenticator.description: If enabled, the RADIUS client requires the RADIUS server to include a Message-Authenticator attribute in all its responses. The client also includes a Message-Authenticator attribute in all Access-Request packets it sends to the server.

healthCheckInterval.title: Health Check Interval
healthCheckInterval.description: The interval between checks to unavailable RADIUS servers, in minutes. Determines how often AM checks an offline server's status. The check sends an invalid authentication request to the RADIUS server. Offline servers are not used until the healthcheck is successful. Primary servers that become available are used before secondary servers.

RecoveryCodeCollectorDecisionNode.name: Recovery Code Collector Decision
RecoveryCodeCollectorDecisionNode.help: Verifies a user's recovery code provided when registering a device for multi-factor authentication.

recoveryCodeType.title: Recovery Code Type
recoveryCodeType.description: Determines which type of recovery codes are going to be validated for the user.

RecoveryCodeDisplayNode.name: Recovery Code Display Node
RecoveryCodeDisplayNode.help: Retrieves generated recovery codes from the transient state and presents them to the user, for safe-keeping.

RegisterLogoutWebhookNode.name: Register Logout Webhook
RegisterLogoutWebhookNode.help: Registers the specified webhook to trigger when a user's session ends.

webhookName.title: Webhook Name
webhookName.description: The name of the webhook stored using the webhook service.

RemoveSessionPropertiesNode.name: Remove Session Properties
RemoveSessionPropertiesNode.help: Removes the specified properties from a user's session data.

propertyNames.title: Property Names
propertyNames.description: The names of session properties to remove that may have been contributed by nodes that executedearlier in the tree. If the properties do not exist, no error will be thrown. Names are case sensitive.

RequestHeaderNode.name: Request Header Node
RequestHeaderNode.help: Gets headers from the request and sets them into the sharedState.

allowedHeaders.title: Allowed headers
allowedHeaders.description: A list of key-value pairs. KEY is the header name and VALUE is the shared state property name.

headersToBeDelimited.title: Allowed headers to be delimited
headersToBeDelimited.description: A list of the "Allowed headers" that can take multiple values, and whose values you want to store in the shared state in a comma-delimited list. If you don't delimit the values of a multi-valued header, the node stores the values in the shared state as a single string value.

RetryLimitDecisionNode.name: Retry Limit Decision
RetryLimitDecisionNode.help: Allows the specified number of passes through to the Retry outcome path, before continuing tree evaluation along the Reject outcome path.

retryLimit.title: Retry Limit
retryLimit.description: The number of times to allow a retry.

incrementUserAttributeOnFailure.title: Save Retry Limit to User
incrementUserAttributeOnFailure.description: If true the number of failures will be persisted beyond the scope of the execution of this tree by saving them to an attribute on the user. If no user can be identified as part of the tree context then the execution of the tree will end with an error. If this is false then failures will be only be stored in the context of the current tree execution and will be lost if the tree execution is restarted.

ScriptedDecisionNode.name: Scripted Decision
ScriptedDecisionNode.help: Executes a script and continues along the path matching the result.

script.title: Script

enum_titles: Next Generation Scripted Decision Node Script
Device Profile Match Template - Decision Node Script

enumNames: Next Generation Scripted Decision Node Script
Device Profile Match Template - Decision Node Script

outputs.title: Script Outputs
outputs.description: A list of state outputs produced by the script.

inputs.title: Script Inputs
inputs.description: A list of state inputs required by the script.

SetCustomCookieNode.name: Set Custom Cookie
SetCustomCookieNode.help: Creates a custom cookie with supplied properties. The Cookie is set in the next response to client.

value.title: Custom Cookie Value
value.description: Sets the value of the custom cookie.

sameSite.title: Custom Cookie SameSite attribute
sameSite.description:
Sets the same site attribute of the custom cookie. This value controls when a browser sends the cookie in cross-site requests. Possible values include:
* `STRICT`: The cookie is only sent when navigating within the same site. It's not sent on any cross-site requests, even for top-level navigations.
* `LAX`: The cookie is sent on same-site requests and top-level navigations. It's not sent on subresource requests (like images or scripts loaded from a third-party site).
* `NONE`: There are no restrictions on the cookie domain.

domain.title: Custom Cookie Domain
domain.description: Sets the domain of the custom cookie.

useSecureCookie.title: Use Secure Cookie
useSecureCookie.description: Sets the custom cookie as "Secure". This will be ignored and set to true if SameSite is set to "NONE".

maxAge.title: Max Age
maxAge.description: The maximum length of time the custom cookie is valid for, in seconds. Both the Max Age and Expires attribute of the cookie are set behind the scenes for compatibility purposes.

name.title: Custom Cookie Name
name.description: Sets the name of the custom cookie.

path.title: Custom Cookie Path
path.description: Sets the path of the custom cookie.

SetErrorDetailsNode.help: Set a localized message and additional details to return to the user when an error occurs

errorMessage.title: Error Message
errorMessage.description: The localizable message to add to the response body when an error occurs

headerControl.title: Response Headers
headerControl.description: The headers to set on the response when an error occurs.

errorDetails.title: Error Details
errorDetails.description: The details to add to the response body when an error occurs

SetFailureDetailsNode.name: Set Failure Details
SetFailureDetailsNode.help: Set a localized message and additional details to return to the user when the authentication fails

headerControl.title: Response Headers
headerControl.description: The headers to set on the response when authentication fails.

failureMessage.title: Failure Message
failureMessage.description: The localizable message to add to the response body when the authentication fails

failureDetails.title: Failure Details
failureDetails.description: The details to add to the response body when the authentication fails

SetFailureUrlNode.name: Failure URL
SetFailureUrlNode.help: Sets the URL to be redirected to when authentication fails.

SetLogoutDetailsNode.name: Set Logout Details
SetLogoutDetailsNode.help: Registers the details to add to the response body when the user logs out.

logoutDetails.title: Logout Details
logoutDetails.description: The details to add to the response body when the user logs out.

SetSessionPropertiesNode.name: Set Session Properties
SetSessionPropertiesNode.help: Sets additional key, value properties to the user's session data.

maxSessionTime.title: Maximum Session Time
maxSessionTime.description: Maximum time a session can remain valid before AM requires the user to authenticate again, in minutes. If provided, this property overrides the corresponding setting at the global, realm, or journey level.

properties.title: Properties
properties.description: The properties to set on the user's session when it is created.

maxIdleTime.title: Maximum Idle Time
maxIdleTime.description: Maximum time a session can remain idle before AM requires the user to authenticate again, in minutes. If provided, this property overrides the corresponding setting at the global, realm, or journey level.

SetStateNode.name: Set State
SetStateNode.help: Adds specified attributes to the state. Do not use for sensitive data.

attributes.title: Attributes
attributes.description: Attributes to add to the state. Do not enter any sensitive data.

SetSuccessDetailsNode.name: Set Success Details
SetSuccessDetailsNode.help: Set additional details to return to the user when authentication succeeds. The details set can't override the predefined keys: tokenId, successURL, or realm.

sessionProperties.title: Session Properties
sessionProperties.description: Session properties map to add to response body when authentication succeeds. This is similar to Success Details except that the values must be keys that are present in the session as properties. The keys will be mapped to values and added to the response body. If the property is not present in the session, it will not be added to the response body.

successDetails.title: Success Details
successDetails.description: The static details to add to the response body when authentication succeeds.

headerControl.title: Response Headers
headerControl.description: The headers to set on the response when authentication succeeds.

SetSuccessUrlNode.name: Success URL
SetSuccessUrlNode.help: Sets the URL to be redirected to when authentication succeeds.

openIdValidationMethod.title: OpenID Connect Validation Type
openIdValidationMethod.description:
In order to validate the ID token from the OpenID Connect provider, the node needs either a URL to get the public keys for the provider, or the symmetric key for an ID token signed with a HMAC-based algorithm.

By default, the configuration type is .well-known/openid-configuration_url. This means the node should retrieve the keys based on information in the OpenID Connect Provider Configuration Document.

You can instead configure the authentication node to validate the ID token signature with the client secret key you provide, or to validate the ID token with the keys retrieved from the URL to the OpenID Connect provider's JSON web key set.
