issuer.title: Token Issuer
issuer.description: Required when the 'openid' scope is included. Value must match the iss field in issued ID Token e.g. accounts.google.com The issuer value MUST be provided when OAuth 2.0 Mix-Up Mitigation is enabled.

cfgAccountMapperConfiguration.title: Account Mapper Configuration
cfgAccountMapperConfiguration.description: Mapping of OAuth account to local OpenAM account Attribute configuration that will be used to map the account of the user authenticated in the OAuth 2.0 Provider to the local data store in the OpenAM. Example: OAuth2.0_attribute=local_attribute

clientSecret.title: Client Secret
clientSecret.description:
OAuth client_secret parameter

For more information on the OAuth client_id parameter refer to the RFC 6749 (http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6749#section-2.3.1), section 2.3.1.

scopeString.title: OAuth Scope
scopeString.description:
List of user profile properties

According to the OAuth 2.0 Authorization Framework, scope is a space-separated list of user profile attributes that the client application requires. The list depends on the permissions that the resource owner grants to the client application. Some authorization servers use non-standard separators for scopes.

cfgAccountProviderClass.title: Account Provider
cfgAccountProviderClass.description: Name of the class implementing the account provider. This class is used by the node to find the account from the attributes mapped by the Account Mapper org.forgerock.openam.authentication.modules.common.mapping.AccountProvider interface. String constructor parameters can be provided by appending | separated values.

tokenEndpoint.title: Access Token Endpoint URL
tokenEndpoint.description: OAuth access token endpoint URL This is the URL endpoint for access token retrieval provided by the OAuth Identity Provider.Refer to the RFC 6749 (http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6749#section-3.2), section 3.2.

cfgAccountMapperClass.title: Account Mapper
cfgAccountMapperClass.description: Name of the class implementing the attribute mapping for the account search. This class is used by the node to map from the account information received from the OAuth Identity Provider into OpenAM. The class must implement the org.forgerock.openam.authentication.modules.common.mapping.AttributeMapper interface. Provided implementations are: org.forgerock.openam.authentication.modules.common.mapping.JsonAttributeMapper org.forgerock.openam.authentication.modules.oidc.JwtAttributeMapper (canonly be used when using the openid scope) String constructor parameters can be provided by appending | separated values.

userInfoEndpoint.title: User Profile Service URL
userInfoEndpoint.description:
User profile information URL

This URL endpoint provides user profile information and is provided by the OAuth Identity Provider NB This URL should return JSON objects in response

If this field is left empty, attributes will be mapped from claims returned on the id_token and no call to the UserInfo endpoint will be made.

openIdValidationValue.title: OpenID Connect Validation Value
openIdValidationValue.description:
Specifies the full URL to the discovery or JWK location, corresponding to the configuration type selected in the OpenID Connect validation configuration type property.

Example: https://accounts.google.com/.well-known/openid-configuration

cfgAttributeMappingClasses.title: Attribute Mapper
cfgAttributeMappingClasses.description: Name of the class that implements the attribute mapping This class maps the OAuth properties into OpenAM properties. A custom attribute mapper can be provided. A custom attribute mapper must implement the org.forgerock.openam.authentication.modules.common.mapping.AttributeMapper interface. Provided implementations are: org.forgerock.openam.authentication.modules.common.mapping.JsonAttributeMapper org.forgerock.openam.authentication.modules.oidc.JwtAttributeMapper (can only be used when using the openid scope)

startTimeProperty.title: Start Time Property
startTimeProperty.description: Identifier of property into which start time should be stored by this node.

TimerStopNode.name: Timer Stop
TimerStopNode.help: Records the time elapsed since tree evaluation passed through the specified Timer Start Node in the specified metric name.

metricKey.title: Metric Key
metricKey.description: Identifier of metric to update when this node is processed.

startTimeProperty.title: Start Time Property
startTimeProperty.description: Identifier of property in which start time should have been stored by a "Timer Start" node.

UpdateJourneyTimeoutNode.name: Update Journey Timeout
UpdateJourneyTimeoutNode.help: Updates the timeout for the current journey.

value.title: Value
value.description:
The value in minutes.
If the operation is Set, this becomes the new timeout so the value must be a positive integer. If the operation is Modify, this value is added to the current timeout value. Modify accepts negative values to subtract minutes from the current timeout value.

operation.title: Operation
operation.description: The timeout update operation to perform. This can either be Set or Modify.

UsernameCollectorNode.name: Username Collector
UsernameCollectorNode.help: Prompts the user to enter their username.

autocompleteValues.title: Autocomplete Values
autocompleteValues.description: If set, the input field will provide autocomplete suggestions based on these values, as per HTML 5 specifications.

WebAuthnAuthenticationNode.name: WebAuthn Authentication Node
WebAuthnAuthenticationNode.help: Allows users of supported clients to use a registered FIDO device during authentication.

timeout.title: Timeout
timeout.description: The number of seconds to wait for a valid WebAuthn authenticator to be registered before failing.

origins.title: Origin domains
origins.description: A set of fully-qualified URLs of accepted origins, e.g. http://app.example.com:443. If empty, the accepted origin is the incoming request origin.

detectSignCountMismatch.title: Detect sign count mismatch
detectSignCountMismatch.description: If enabled, the node compares the authenticator's sign count (signature counter) with the sign count stored in the user's profile. If the authenticator sign count is less than or equal to the stored value, the node outputs to the Sign Count Mismatch outcome. The node is considered to have succeeded even if this outcome is used. The outcome is useful for detecting cloned or malfunctioning authenticators.

requiresResidentKey.title: Username from device
requiresResidentKey.description: Requests that the username is selected by the device. Devices which do not support storing and providing the username will be unable to utilise the node while it is operating in this mode.

asScript.title: Return challenge as JavaScript
asScript.description: If enabled, the node will return its challenge as a fully encapsulated client-side JavaScript that will interact directly with the WebAuthn API and submit the response back. If disabled, the node will return the challenge and associated data in a metadata node, and the custom UI will use that to interact with the WebAuthn API itself.

userVerificationRequirement.title: User verification requirement
userVerificationRequirement.description: If specified as REQUIRED, authenticators that don't verify user identity are filtered out and should not be selectable by the user.

relyingPartyDomain.title: Relying party identifier
relyingPartyDomain.description: The domain against which to register devices, if blank AM will make a best guess at the domain.

WebAuthnDeviceStorageNode.name: WebAuthn Device Storage Node
WebAuthnDeviceStorageNode.help: Stores devices which have previously been saved into the transient state by the WebAuthnRegistrationNode.

generateRecoveryCodes.title: Generate recovery codes
generateRecoveryCodes.description: If enabled, the success outcome's transient state will contain a set of recovery codes. If this success outcome is passed into a Recovery Code Display Node, these codes will be presented to the user. A user may use recovery codes to bypass the WebAuthn authentication node in the event they have lost their authenticator. A set of recovery codes is shared among all registered WebAuthn authenticators, with the latest-generated set being the only valid set of codes.

maxSavedDevices.title: Maximum Saved Devices
maxSavedDevices.description: Specify the maximum number of WebAuthn devices to save in a user's profile. 0 for unlimited.

WebAuthnRegistrationNode.name: WebAuthn Registration Node
WebAuthnRegistrationNode.help: Allows users of supported clients to register FIDO2 devices for use during authentication.

validateFidoU2fAaguid.title: Validate FIDO-U2F attestation AAGUID
validateFidoU2fAaguid.description: If enabled, the node will validate the AAGUID for any FIDO-U2F attestation type (16 bytes initialized with all zeros).

storeAttestationDataInTransientState.title: Store data in transient state
storeAttestationDataInTransientState.description: If enabled, the information provided by the device to the node will be stored in the transient state for later analysis by subsequent nodes using the key 'webauthnData'. Additionally the type of attestation achieved (BASIC, CA, SELF, etc.) will be stored using the key 'webauthnAttestationType'.

origins.title: Origin domains
origins.description: A set of fully-qualified URLs of accepted origins, e.g. http://app.example.com:443. If empty, the accepted origin is the incoming request origin.

maxSavedDevices.title: Maximum Saved Devices
maxSavedDevices.description: Specify the maximum number of WebAuthn devices to save in a user's profile. 0 for unlimited.

generateRecoveryCodes.title: Generate recovery codes
generateRecoveryCodes.description: If enabled, the success outcome's transient state will contain a set of recovery codes. If this success outcome is passed into a Recovery Code Display Node, these codes will be presented to the user. A user may use recovery codes to bypass the WebAuthn authentication node in the event they have lost their authenticator. A set of recovery codes is shared among all registered WebAuthn authenticators, with the latest-generated set being the only valid set of codes. This will not occur if the option to store the device data in the transient state is also selected.

enforceRevocationCheck.title: Enforce revocation check
enforceRevocationCheck.description: Whether to enforce the checking of revocation entries from certificates. If this is set to true, then any attestation certificate's trust chain MUST have a CRL or OCSP entry that can be verified by AM during processing. If this is set to false, then presented certificates will not be checked for revocation. Certificates downloaded from the FIDO Metadata Service may not have a CRL/OCSP entry.

timeout.title: Timeout
timeout.description: The number of seconds to wait for a valid WebAuthn authenticator to be registered before failing.

excludeCredentials.title: Limit registrations
excludeCredentials.description: If enabled, each authenticator may only be registered against a user's profile once.

trustStoreAlias.title: Trust Store alias
trustStoreAlias.description: The alias of the realm trust store which contains the secrets necessary for performing validation of a supplied attestation certificate. The alias name must only contain the characters a-z and the . symbol.

relyingPartyDomain.title: Relying party identifier
relyingPartyDomain.description: The domain against which to register devices, if blank AM will make a best guess at the domain.

asScript.title: Return challenge as JavaScript
asScript.description: If enabled, the node will return its challenge as a fully encapsulated client-side JavaScript that will interact directly with the WebAuthn API and submit the response back. If disabled, the node will return the challenge and associated data in a metadata node, and the custom UI will use that to interact with the WebAuthn API itself.

relyingPartyName.title: Relying party
relyingPartyName.description: The name of the Relying Party to present, this could be the name of the organisation, realm, etc.

postponeDeviceProfileStorage.title: Store device data in transient state
postponeDeviceProfileStorage.description: If enabled, the device will not be stored directly to the user profile upon successful completion of the node. Rather, the device information will be placed into the transient state for later storage by subsequent nodes using the key 'webauthnDeviceData'. The provided 'WebAuthn Device Storage Node' can be used for this purpose.

displayNameSharedState.title: Shared state attribute for display name
displayNameSharedState.description: This field determines the value of the user's displayName, used when the user's username is stored in the device. If left blank, the display name will be set to the same as the user's username. If set to a value the corresponding shared state value will be used instead. If there is no value found in the shared state for the provided key, the display name will be set to the same as the user's username.

requiresResidentKey.title: Username to device
requiresResidentKey.description: Requests that the username is stored by the device. Devices which do not support storing and providing the username will be unable to utilise the node while it is operating in this mode.

userVerificationRequirement.title: User verification requirement
userVerificationRequirement.description: If specified as REQUIRED, authenticators that don't verify user identity are filtered out and should not be selectable by the user.

fidoCertificationLevel.title: FIDO Certification Level
fidoCertificationLevel.description: The minimum FIDO certification level that the device's certification status must satisfy during a registration flow. If set to OFF, no metadata service checks are performed. NOTE: To enforce a FIDO-certified check, the WebAuthN Metadata Service must be configured appropriately for the realm.

authenticatorAttachment.title: Authentication attachment
authenticatorAttachment.description: If specified, the authenticators will be filtered out that don't match the attachment type. A PLATFORM authenticator is part of the device, and CROSS_PLATFORM authenticator can be removed from a device and used elsewhere, e.g. via USB.

ZeroPageLoginNode.name: Zero Page Login Collector
ZeroPageLoginNode.help: Checks whether selected headers are provided in the incoming authentication request, and if so, uses their value as the provided username and password.

product-CaptchaEnterpriseNode.name: reCAPTCHA Enterprise
product-CaptchaEnterpriseNode.help: Enables the use of Google reCAPTCHA Enterprise in journeys.

siteKey.title: reCAPTCHA Site Key
siteKey.description:
The ID of the reCAPTCHA key you created in the Google Cloud console.
The key can be for any platform type, "Website", "Android app", or "iOS app".
Sometimes referred to as a "key ID" in the Google Cloud console and documentation.

divClass.title: reCAPTCHA CSS class
divClass.description:
A CSS class to apply to the HTML elements that reCAPTCHA adds to JavaScript apps.
Default is g-recaptcha.

secretLabelIdentifier.title: reCAPTCHA API key secret identifier
secretLabelIdentifier.description:
An identifier used to create a secret label for mapping to your Google reCAPTCHA API key in a secret store.
Get or create your API key from the Google Cloud Console under APIs and Services > Credentials.
The label this property creates takes the form am.authentication.nodes.captchaEnterprise.{{identifier}}.secret where {{identifier}} is the string you specify.
The string can only contain characters {{a-z}} {{A-Z}} {{0-9}} {{.}} and cannot start or end with {{.}}.

projectId.title: Google Cloud project ID
projectId.description:
The ID of the project that has Google reCAPTCHA enabled.
You can get the ID of your project in the Google Cloud console.
For example, my-project-65746-07969469388.

storeErrors.title: Store reCAPTCHA error messages
storeErrors.description:
Stores the error messages for future reference within the journey.
The node stores the error messages in the CaptchaEnterpriseNode.FAILURE variable.

storeAssessmentResult.title: Store reCAPTCHA assessment JSON
storeAssessmentResult.description:
Stores the assessment response JSON for future reference within the journey.
The node stores the JSON response in the CaptchaEnterpriseNode.ASSESSMENT_RESULT variable.

apiUri.title: JavaScript reCAPTCHA API URL
apiUri.description:
The URL of the JavaScript file containing the reCAPTCHA API.
Only change this if Google release a new version of the JavaScript reCAPTCHA API.
Default is https://www.google.com/recaptcha/enterprise.js.

scoreThreshold.title: Score threshold
scoreThreshold.description:
Specifies the score threshold for determining if a user is likely to be a real person.
reCAPTCHA scores are between 0.0 and 1.0, with higher scores indicating higher confidence that the user is a real person.
If the returned score is equal to or greater than the threshold the journey continues along the true outcome path.

captchaUri.title: reCAPTCHA verification URL
captchaUri.description:
The URL to send the reCAPTCHA to for verification.
Only change this if Google update the URL used for reCAPTCHA verifications.
Default is https://recaptchaenterprise.googleapis.com/v1.

product-CaptchaNode.name: CAPTCHA
product-CaptchaNode.help: Verifies the response token received from the CAPTCHA verifier in addition to creating a CAPTCHA callback for the UI to interact with, default values are for Google ReCAPTCHA.

reCaptchaV3.title: ReCaptcha V3 Node
reCaptchaV3.description: If this is selected then a frictionless captcha will be assumed, leaving no interactive Captcha elements on the page.

scoreThreshold.title: Score Threshold
scoreThreshold.description: Specifies the score threshold for determining if a user is likely to be a real person. CAPTCHA scores received will be between 0.0 and 1.0. The higher the score, the more likely the user is a real person. This score is not provided by all CAPTCHA implementations, please check the documentation for your vendor.

divClass.title: Class of CAPTCHA HTML Element
divClass.description: The class of the HTML element required by the captcha API, defaults to the value for Google ReCAPTCHA.

captchaUri.title: CAPTCHA Verification URL
captchaUri.description: URL to Verify CAPTCHA, defaults to the Google ReCAPTCHA verification URI.

secretKey.title: CAPTCHA Secret Key
secretKey.description: CAPTCHA Secret Key. This property is deprecated. Use the CAPTCHA Secret Label Identifier instead. If you set a CAPTCHA Secret Label Identifier and AM finds a matching secret in a secret store, the CAPTCHA Secret Key is ignored.

apiUri.title: CAPTCHA API URL
apiUri.description: The URL of the JavaScript to load the CAPTCHA verification, defaults to the Google ReCAPTCHA API.

disableSubmission.title: Disable submission until verified
disableSubmission.description: If this is selected, form submission is disabled until CAPTCHA verification succeeds.

secretLabelIdentifier.title: CAPTCHA Secret Label Identifier
secretLabelIdentifier.description:
Identifier used to create a secret label for mapping to a secret in a secret store.
AM uses this identifier to create a specific secret label for this node. The secret label takes the form am.authentication.nodes.captcha.{{identifier}}.secret where {{identifier}} is the value of CAPTCHA Secret Label Identifier. The identifier can only contain characters {{a-z}} {{A-Z}} {{0-9}} {{.}} and cannot start or end with {{.}}. If you set a CAPTCHA Secret Label Identifier and AM finds a matching secret in a secret store, the CAPTCHA Secret Key is ignored.

certificateCollectionMethod.title: Certificate Collection Method
certificateCollectionMethod.description: This field defines how the certificate is collected from the request. If TLS termination happens at the web container that is running Access Management, choose Request. If you have Access Management behind a proxy or load balancer and terminate TLS there, select Header. If Either is selected, the collector node will first look at the request, then look at the HTTP Header Name for Client Certificate specified in that order.

trustedRemoteHosts.title: Trusted Remote Hosts
trustedRemoteHosts.description:
A list of IP addresses trusted to supply client certificates.

If SSL/TLS is being terminated at a load balancer or at the Distributed Authentication server then this option can be used to ensure that only specified trusted hosts (identified by IP address) are allowed to supply client certificates to the certificate node.

Empty list means do not trust remote headers and a single value of "any" means all are trusted any.

clientCertificateHttpHeaderName.title: HTTP Header Name for Client Certificate
clientCertificateHttpHeaderName.description: The name of the HTTP request header containing the certificate, only used when header based collection is enabled.

otherCertificateAttributeToProfileMappin.title: Other Certificate Field Used to Access User Profile
otherCertificateAttributeToProfileMappin.description: This field is only used if the Certificate Field Used to Access User Profile attribute is set to other. This field allows a custom certificate field to be used as the basis of the user search.

certificateAttributeToProfileMapping.title: Certificate Field Used to Access User Profile
certificateAttributeToProfileMapping.description: The certificate node needs to read a value from the client certificate that can be used to search the LDAP server for the user. This value from the certificate will be populated in shared state under the username key.

certificateAttributeProfileMappingExtens.title: SubjectAltNameExt Value Type to Access User Profile
certificateAttributeProfileMappingExtens.description:
Use the Subject Alternative Name Field in preference to one of the standard certificate fields.

Selecting RFC822Name or UPN will cause this field to have have precedence over the Certificate Field Used to Access User Profile or Other Certificate Field Used to Access User Profile attribute.

ldapCertificateAttribute.title: Subject DN Attribute Used to Search LDAP for Certificates
ldapCertificateAttribute.description:
This is the attribute used to search the directory for the certificate.

The Certificate node will search the directory for the certificate using the search filter based on this attribute and the value of the Subject DN taken from the certificate.

userBindPassword.title: LDAP Server Authentication Password
userBindPassword.description: The password for the authentication user. If mTLS is enabled, this attribute is ignored.

matchCertificateInLdap.title: Match Certificate in LDAP
matchCertificateInLdap.description: The client certificate must exist in the directory for the authentication to be successful.

certificateIdentityStore.title: Certificate Identity Store
certificateIdentityStore.description:
Select the realm identity store where AM will search for certificates.

If you select an identity store here, AM uses the connection details defined for that identity store and ignores all the server settings below this field.

crlMatchingCertificateAttribute.title: Issuer DN Attribute(s) Used to Search LDAP for CRLs
crlMatchingCertificateAttribute.description:
This is the name of the attribute taken from the CA certificate that will be used to search the CRL.

If only one attribute name is specified, the ldap search filter will be (attrName=Value_of_the_corresponding_Attribute_from_SubjectDN) e.g. SubjectDN of issuer cert 'C=US, CN=Some CA, serialNumber=123456',attribute name specified is 'CN', search filter used will be (CN=Some CA).

If several attribute names are specified, they have to separated by ,. The resulting ldap search filter value will be a comma separated list of name attribute values, the search attribute will be cn e.g. SubjectDN of issuer cert 'C=US, CN=Some CA, serialNumber=123456',attribute names specified are 'CN, serialNumber', search filter used will be cn=CN=Some CA,serialNumber=123456. The order of the values of the attribute names matteras they must match the value of the cn attribute of a crlDistributionPoint entry in the directory server.

matchCertificateToCRL.title: Match Certificate to CRL
matchCertificateToCRL.description:
The Client Certificate will be checked against the Certificate Revocation list held in thedirectory.

A Certificate Revocation List can be provisioned into the directory. Having this option enabled will cause all client certificates to be checked against this list.

checkCertificateExpiry.title: Check Certificate Expiration
checkCertificateExpiry.description: Check to see if the certificate is expired.

mtlsSecretLabel.title: mTLS Secret Label Identifier
mtlsSecretLabel.description:
Label identifier used to create a secret label for mapping to the mTLS certificate in the secret store.
AM uses this identifier to create a specific secret label for this node. The secret label takes the form am.authentication.nodes.certificate.validation.mtls.{{identifier}}.cert where {{identifier}} is the value of mTLS Secret Label Identifier. The label identifier can only contain characters {{a-z}} {{A-Z}} {{0-9}} {{.}} and cannot start or end with {{.}}.

mtlsEnabled.title: mTLS Enabled
mtlsEnabled.description:
Enables mTLS (mutual TLS) between AM and this store. When mTLS is enabled:
  • Set SSL enabled to true.
  • The values for LDAP Server Authentication User and LDAP Server Authentication Password are ignored.
  • You must provide an mTLS Secret Label Identifier.
Instructions for setting up certificates and keystore mappings are in the product documentation.

cacheCRLsInMemory.title: Cache CRLs in Memory
cacheCRLsInMemory.description: The CRLs will be cached in memory.

userBindDN.title: LDAP Server Authentication User
userBindDN.description:
DN of the user used by the node to authenticate to the LDAP server.

The Certificate node authenticates to the LDAP server in order to search for a matching certificate. The DN entered here represents the account used for said authentication and must have read/search access to the LDAP server. If mTLS is enabled, this attribute is ignored.

ocspValidationEnabled.title: OCSP Validation
ocspValidationEnabled.description:
Enable Online Certificate Status Protocol validation for OCSP aware certificates.

If the certificate contains OCSP validation information then OpenAM will use this information to check thevalidity of the certificate as part of the authentication process.

The OpenAM server must have Internet connectivity for OCSP to work.

sslEnabled.title: Use SSL/TLS for LDAP Access
sslEnabled.description: The certificate node will use SSL/TLS to access the LDAP server.

certificateLdapServers.title: LDAP Server Where Certificates are Stored
certificateLdapServers.description:
Use this list to set the LDAP server used to search for certificates.

The Certificate authentication node will use this list for the LDAP server used to search for certificates. A single entry must be in the format:
ldap_server:port

Multiple entries allow associations between OpenAM servers and a LDAP server. The format is:
local server name | server:port

The local server name is the full name of the server from the list of servers and sites.

updateCRLsFromDistributionPoint.title: Update CA CRLs from CRLDistributionPoint
updateCRLsFromDistributionPoint.description:
Fetch new CA CRLs from CRLDistributionPoint and update it in Directory Server.

If the CA certificate includes an IssuingDistributionPoint or has an CRLDistributionPoint extension set OpenAM tries to update the CRLs if needed (i.e. CRL is out-of-date).
This property controls if the update should be performed.
This property is only used if CA CRL checking is enabled.

crlHttpParameters.title: HTTP Parameters for CRL Update
crlHttpParameters.description:
These parameters will be included in any HTTP CRL call to the Certificate Authority.

If the Client or CA certificate contains the Issuing Distribution Point Extension then OpenAM will use this information to retrieve the CRL from the distribution point. This property allow custom HTTP parameters to be included in the CRL request.

The format of the parameter is as follows:
param1=value1,param2=value

ldapSearchStartDN.title: LDAP Search Start or Base DN
ldapSearchStartDN.description:
The start point in the LDAP server for the certificate and CRL search.

When entering multiple entries, each entry must be prefixed with a local server name. Multiple entries allow different search Base DNs depending on the OpenAM server in use. The format is:
local server name | base dn

The local server name is the full name of the server from the list of servers and sites.

product-KerberosNode.name: Kerberos Node
product-KerberosNode.help: Enables Windows desktop single sign on such that a user who has already authenticated with a Kerberos Key Distribution Center can authenticate to AM

returnPrincipalWithDomainName.title: Return Principal with Domain Name
returnPrincipalWithDomainName.description: Returns the fully qualified name of the authenticated user rather than just the username.

kerberosServerName.title: Kerberos Server Name
kerberosServerName.description: The hostname/IP address of the Kerberos (Active Directory) server.

trustedKerberosRealms.title: Trusted Kerberos realms
trustedKerberosRealms.description: List of Trusted Kerberos Realms for User Kerberos tickets.

principalName.title: Service Principal
principalName.description:
The name of the Kerberos principal used during authentication. The format of the field is as follows:

HTTP/openam.forgerock.com@AD_DOMAIN.COM

lookupUserInRealm.title: Lookup User In Realm
lookupUserInRealm.description: Validate that the user has a matched user profile configured in the data store.

keytabFileName.title: Key Tab File Path
keytabFileName.description: The absolute pathname of the AD keytab file.

kerberosRealm.title: Kerberos Realm
kerberosRealm.description: The name of the Kerberos (Active Directory) realm used for authentication.

kerberosServiceIsInitiator.title: Is Initiator
kerberosServiceIsInitiator.description: True, if initiator. False, if acceptor only. Default is True.

product-PingOneProtectEvaluationNode.name: PingOne Protect Evaluation
product-PingOneProtectEvaluationNode.help: Risk evaluations calculate the risk level and other risk-related details associated with an event.

customAttributes.title: Node State Attribute For Custom Attributes
customAttributes.description:
The node state variable that contains the custom attributes map for PingOne Protect.

If left blank, no custom attributes are sent in the request.

storeEvaluateResult.title: Store Risk Evaluation
storeEvaluateResult.description:
Stores the risk evaluation response in node state under a key named PingOneProtectEvaluationNode.RISK.

Note: The key is empty if the node is unable to retrieve a risk evaluation from PingOne.

sessionId.title: Node State Attribute For Event Session ID
sessionId.description:
The node state variable that contains the Session ID to use in the risk evaluation event sent to PingOne Protect.

This session ID is used to help track requests sent to PingOne. If left blank, the PingAM audit tracking ID for the session is used.

userId.title: Node State Attribute For User ID
userId.description:
The node state variable that contains the user.id value to send to PingOne Protect.

If left blank, the node uses the current context UserId as the user.id.

nodeStateAttributeForSubtype.title: Node State Attribute For Flow Subtype
nodeStateAttributeForSubtype.description: The node state attribute containing the flow subtype for which the risk evaluation is being carried out.If set, this value takes precedence over the Authentication Flow Subtype and Authorization Flow Subtype.

deviceSharingType.title: Device Sharing Type
deviceSharingType.description:
Whether the device is shared between users or not.

Choose from UNSPECIFIED, SHARED, or PRIVATE.

dynamicTargetResourceID.title: Use Node State Attribute For Target App ID
dynamicTargetResourceID.description:
Setting this to true instructs the node to get the target application ID from node state using the value of Target App ID.

This only applies if Target App ID is set.

recommendedActions.title: Recommended Actions
recommendedActions.description: A list of recommended actions the risk evaluation could return. Each entry in the list becomes a node outcome. If the score does not exceed the threshold and a recommended action is present in the evaluation, the journey continues down the matching entry in this list.

dynamicTargetResourceName.title: Use Node State Attribute For Target App Name
dynamicTargetResourceName.description:
Setting this to true instructs the node to get the target application name from node state using the value of Target App Name.

This only applies if Target App Name is set.

targetResourceID.title: Target App ID
targetResourceID.description:
The ID of the target application.

If you enable "Use Node State Attribute For Target App ID", the node uses this value as a key to retrieve the required value from node state. Otherwise, the node uses the literal value provided in this field.

scoreThreshold.title: Score Threshold
scoreThreshold.description: Scoring higher than this value results in evaluation continuing along the Exceeds Score Threshold outcome.

dynamicRiskPolicySetID.title: Use Node State Attribute For Risk Policy Set ID
dynamicRiskPolicySetID.description:
Setting this to true instructs the node to get the risk policy set ID from node state using the value of Risk Policy Set ID.

This only applies if Risk Policy Set ID is set.

If a value corresponding to the key provided in "Risk Policy Set ID" can't be found, no value is sent to PingOne and the default risk policy is applied.

userGroups.title: Node State Attribute For User Groups
userGroups.description:
The node state variable that contains the list of group names to send to PingOne Protect.

If left blank, the node sends the user groups from the current context identity.

targetedPoliciesEvaluation.title: Targeted Policies Evaluation
targetedPoliciesEvaluation.description: If enabled, the risk evaluation uses the targeted policies defined in the PingOne user environment rather than a specific policy.

flowType.title: Flow Type
flowType.description:
The type of flow or event for which the risk evaluation is being carried out. Choose from:
  • REGISTRATION - initial registration of an account
  • AUTHENTICATION - standard authentication for login or actions such as password change
  • ACCESS - verification of whether user can access the relevant application
  • AUTHORIZATION - verification of whether user is authorized to perform a specific action such as a profile change
  • TRANSACTION - authentication carried out in the context of a purchase or other one-time transaction

targetResourceName.title: Target App Name
targetResourceName.description:
The name of the target application. This is only used if Target App ID is set.

If you enable "Use Node State Attribute For Target App Name", the node uses this value as a key to retrieve the required value from node state. Otherwise, the node uses the literal value provided in this field.

deviceExternalId.title: Node State Attribute For Device External ID
deviceExternalId.description:
The node state variable that contains an external device ID to send to PingOne Protect in the evaluation request.

This property lets you send a custom device ID to PingOne Protect in addition to the device ID provided by the Signals SDK.

riskPolicySetID.title: Risk Policy Set ID
riskPolicySetID.description:
The ID of the risk policy set.

If not specified, the environment's default risk policy set is used.

If you enable "Targeted Policies Evaluation", this value is ignored.

If you enable "Use Node State Attribute For Risk Policy Set ID", the node uses this value as a key to retrieve the required value from node state. Otherwise, the node uses the literal value provided in this field.

username.title: Node State Attribute For Username
username.description:
The node state variable that contains the user.name value to send to PingOne Protect.

If left blank, the node uses the current context Username as the user.name.

authenticationFlowSubtype.title: Authentication Flow Subtype
authenticationFlowSubtype.description: If it's an AUTHENTICATION flow, the flow subtype for which the risk evaluation is being carried out.If Node State Attribute For Flow Subtype is set, that value takes precedence.

pauseBehavioralData.title: Pause Behavioral Data
pauseBehavioralData.description: After receiving the device signal, instruct the client to pause collecting behavioral data.

authorizationFlowSubtype.title: Authorization Flow Subtype
authorizationFlowSubtype.description: If it's an AUTHORIZATION flow, the flow subtype for which the risk evaluation is being carried out.If Node State Attribute For Flow Subtype is set, that value takes precedence.

pingOneWorker.title: PingOne Worker Service ID
pingOneWorker.description: The ID of the PingOne worker service for connecting to PingOne.

product-PingOneProtectInitializeNode.name: PingOne Protect Initialize
product-PingOneProtectInitializeNode.help: Initialize the PingOne Protect SDK on the client device, using the configuration provided by the node properties.

disableTags.title: Disable Tags
disableTags.description: When enabled the SDK will collect tag data. When not enabled (the default), tag data is not collected.

additionalSignalsInitializationOptions.title: Additional Signals SDK Initialization Options
additionalSignalsInitializationOptions.description: Map of additional Signals SDK initialization options not covered by existing configuration options. Keys must not duplicate existing options configured in the node.

pingOneWorker.title: PingOne Worker Service ID
pingOneWorker.description: The ID of the PingOne worker service for connecting to PingOne.

enableTrust.title: Enable Universal Device Identification
enableTrust.description: Tie the device payload to a non-extractable crypto key stored on the browser for content authenticity verification.

agentPort.title: Port number for PingID Agent
agentPort.description: The port number to use when connecting to the PingID Device Trust Agent. If left blank, the default port 9400 will be used.

behavioralDataCollection.title: Collect Behavioral Data
behavioralDataCollection.description: When enabled (the default), collect behavioral data. When not enabled, behavioral data is not collected.

agentIdentification.title: Enable PingID Agent Identification
agentIdentification.description: When enabled, the Signals SDK collects attributes from the PingID Device Trust Agent.

agentTimeout.title: Timeout for PingID Agent
agentTimeout.description: The time, in milliseconds, for establishing a connection with the PingID Device Trust Agent. If left blank, the default value is set by the Signals SDK. If specified, the value must be between 200 and 10,000 milliseconds.

product-PingOneProtectResultNode.name: PingOne Protect Result
product-PingOneProtectResultNode.help: Update the risk evaluation configuration, and to modify the completion status of the resource when the risk evaluation is still in progress.

status.title: Completion Status
status.description: The state of the transaction. Options are FAILED and SUCCESS.

product-ReCaptchaNode.name: Legacy CAPTCHA
product-ReCaptchaNode.help: Verifies the response token received from the CAPTCHA verifier in addition to creating a CAPTCHA callback for the UI to interact with, default values are for Google ReCAPTCHA. Note that this has been superceded by the new CAPTCHA node, which should be used in all cases over this node.

divClass.title: Class of CAPTCHA HTML Element
divClass.description: The class of the HTML element required by the captcha API, defaults to the value for Google ReCAPTCHA.

reCaptchaUri.title: CAPTCHA Verification URL
reCaptchaUri.description: URL to Verify CAPTCHA, defaults to the Google ReCAPTCHA verification URI.

apiUri.title: CAPTCHA API URL
apiUri.description: The URL of the JavaScript to load the CAPTCHA verification, defaults to the Google ReCAPTCHA API.

requestBinding.title: Request Binding
requestBinding.description: Use this parameter to indicate what binding the SP should use when communicating with the IdP.

validateIdpEntityId.title: Validate IdP Entity ID
validateIdpEntityId.description: Enable to validate the assertion issuer against the configured IdP Entity ID.

nameIdFormat.title: NameID Format
nameIdFormat.description:
(Optional) Use this parameter to specify a SAML Name Identifier format identifier such as 
urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:nameid-format:persistent
urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:nameid-format:transient
urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:unspecified

authComparison.title: Comparison Type
authComparison.description:
(Optional) Use this parameter to specify a comparison method to evaluate the requested context classes or statements. OpenAM accepts the following values: 
better
, 
exact
, 
maximum
, and 
minimum
.

isPassive.title: Passive Authentication
isPassive.description: Use this parameter to indicate whether the identity provider should authenticate passively (true) or not (false).

authnContextDeclRef.title: Authentication Context Declaration Reference
authnContextDeclRef.description: (Optional) Use this parameter to specify authentication context declaration references.

allowCreate.title: Allow IdP to Create NameID
allowCreate.description: Use this parameter to indicate whether the identity provider can create a new identifier for the principal if none exists (true) or not (false).

idpEntityId.title: IdP Entity ID
idpEntityId.description: The entity name of the SAML2 IdP Service to use for this module (must be configured).

binding.title: Response Binding
binding.description: Use this parameter to indicate what binding the IdP should use when communicating with this SP.

authnContextClassRef.title: Authentication Context Class Reference
authnContextClassRef.description: (Optional) Use this parameter to specify authentication context class references.

metaAlias.title: SP MetaAlias
metaAlias.description:
MetaAlias for Service Provider. The format of this parameter is 
/realm_name/SP

forceAuthn.title: Force IdP Authentication
forceAuthn.description: Use this parameter to indicate whether the identity provider should force authentication (true) or can reuse existing security contexts (false).

product-SecurIdNode.name: RSA SecurID
product-SecurIdNode.help: Allow users to authenticate using RSA SecurID.

clientId.title: Client ID
clientId.description: The string RSA uses to determine principal identity and access control. Optional for the Cloud Authentication Service, required for Authentication Manager.

clientKeySecretLabelIdentifier.title: Client Key Secret Label Identifier
clientKeySecretLabelIdentifier.description: The identifier for the unique key for communication between AM and the RSA API.

assurancePolicy.title: Assurance Policy
assurancePolicy.description: The name of the assurance policy to be used by RSA SecurID. Applies only to the Cloud Authentication Service.

waitingMessage.title: Waiting Message
waitingMessage.description: Localization overrides for the message displayed to the user to respond to a push notification. Key-value map of language to localized message. The value defaults to "Waiting for your response" if no overrides are provided.

authnAttemptTimeout.title: Authentication Attempt Timeout
authnAttemptTimeout.description: The number of seconds before RSA SecurID times out the authentication request. Applies only to the Cloud Authentication Service.

httpClient.title: HTTP Client
httpClient.description: The HTTP Client to use for communication with the RSA API. If you don't have a custom HTTP Client, leave this blank to use the default HTTP Client.

mfaChoicePrompt.title: Prompt for MFA Choice
mfaChoicePrompt.description: Localization overrides for the message displayed to the user when requesting an MFA method. Key-value map of language to localized message. The value defaults to "Select your preferred MFA method" if no overrides are provided.

baseUrl.title: SecurID URL
baseUrl.description: The URL for an instance of RSA Authentication Manager or the RSA Cloud Authentication Service.

product-WriteFederationInformationNode.name: Write Federation Information
product-WriteFederationInformationNode.help: Links the SAML2 authenticated user and their local account together.

AcceptTermsAndConditionsNode.name: Accept Terms and Conditions
AcceptTermsAndConditionsNode.help: Prompts the user to accept or reject the currently active Terms and Conditions.

AttributeCollectorNode.name: Attribute Collector
AttributeCollectorNode.help: Prompts the user to enter any specified attributes.

attributesToCollect.title: Attributes to Collect
attributesToCollect.description: A set of attributes to collect from the client.

identityAttribute.title: Identity Attribute
identityAttribute.description: The attribute used to identify the the object in IDM.

required.title: All Attributes Required
required.description: When set, requires all attributes collected to contain non-null values.

validateInputs.title: Validate Input
validateInputs.description: Set to true if client input should be validated against IDM policy as declared in the schema.

AttributePresentDecisionNode.name: Attribute Present Decision
AttributePresentDecisionNode.help: Checks if an attribute is present on an object regardless of whether the field is private

presentAttribute.title: Present Attribute
presentAttribute.description: The object attribute to verify is present regardless of whether the field is private.

identityAttribute.title: Identity Attribute
identityAttribute.description: The attribute to query in the IDM object.

AttributeValueDecisionNode.name: Attribute Value Decision
AttributeValueDecisionNode.help: Verifies the user's specified attribute matches a specified condition.

identityAttribute.title: Identity Attribute
identityAttribute.description: The attribute to query in the IDM object.

comparisonOperation.title: Comparison Operation
comparisonOperation.description: The operation to perform on the object attribute; PRESENT checks for existence of an attribute, EQUALS checks if the object's attribute value equals the configured comparison value.

comparisonValue.title: Comparison Value
comparisonValue.description: If using the EQUALS comparison operation, the value to compare the object's attribute value to.

BackChannelInitNode.name: Backchannel Initialize
BackChannelInitNode.help: Initialize a separate journey to run asynchronously, possibly by a different user or agent.

customRedirectUrl.title: Custom Redirect URL
customRedirectUrl.description: The custom redirect URL to save to node state. This property is only taken into account if Redirect URL Type is Custom.

timeout.title: Max Time (Seconds)
timeout.description: The number of seconds to wait for the backchannel journey to complete before timing out. If you don't set this, AM uses the default transaction timeout for the realm.

allowRetry.title: Allow Retry
allowRetry.description: If true, the end user can retry the backchannel journey if it fails. There's no maximum _number_ of retries, but the retry window ends after the `Max Time`.

redirectUrlType.title: Redirect URL Type
redirectUrlType.description: The type of redirect URL to save to node state.

idType.title: Subject Type
idType.description: The type of subject (User or Agent) to initialize the journey for. If None, any subject can log in using the generated journey.

usernameKey.title: Subject Name Key
usernameKey.description: The node state key that contains the user ID of the subject to initialize the journey for. This property is ignored if Subject Type is None.

dataKey.title: Data Object Key
dataKey.description: The node state key that contains the data object (if present) to pass to the journey at the root level of shared state.

BackChannelStatusNode.name: Backchannel Status
BackChannelStatusNode.help: Check the status of an asynchronous user journey.

recordData.title: Record Journey Session Info
recordData.description: If true, the node records the session info returned from the journey in the transient state when the journey completes successfully.

ConfigProviderNode.name: Configuration Provider
ConfigProviderNode.help: This scripted node dynamically imitates another node and replaces it in the journey.

script.title: Script
script.description: The configuration provider script to run to generate the configuration for the node.

scriptInputs.title: Script Inputs
scriptInputs.description: A list of inputs required by the configuration script.

nodeType.title: Node Type
nodeType.description: The type of node to imitate. The list is restricted to node types with outcomes that are fixed or variable based on predefined values.

ConsentNode.name: Consent Collector
ConsentNode.help: Prompts the user to provide consent to sharing their profile data.

message.title: Privacy & Consent Message
message.description: Localised message providing the privacy and consent notice.

allRequired.title: All Mappings Required
allRequired.description: All mappings listed by this node require consent in order to move forward.

identityResource.title: Identity Resource
identityResource.description: The identity resource in IDM that this node will create. This is used to aid node input requirement declaration.  Must match identity resource of the current tree.

CreatePasswordNode.help: Allows users to create a password when provisioning an account.

DisplayUserNameNode.name: Display Username
DisplayUserNameNode.help: Presents the user's username for use in username retrieval trees.

userName.title: User Name
userName.description: The attribute used to identify the the user name in IDM.

identityAttribute.title: Identity Attribute
identityAttribute.description: The attribute used to identify the the object in IDM.

EmailSuspendNode.name: Email Suspend Node
EmailSuspendNode.help: Sends an email to the user. The email will include a link to return to this node within the tree.

emailAttribute.title: Email Attribute
emailAttribute.description: The IDM email field to send the email to.

identityAttribute.title: Identity Attribute
identityAttribute.description: The attribute used to identify the the object in IDM.

emailTemplateName.title: Email Template Name
emailTemplateName.description: The IDM email template to be sent.

suspendDuration.title: Suspend Duration (optional)
suspendDuration.description: The duration, in minutes, that the authentication flow is suspended.

emailSuspendMessage.title: Email Suspend Message
emailSuspendMessage.description: The localised message to be returned once the tree is suspended.

default.en: An email has been sent to the address you entered. Click the link in that email to proceed.

objectLookup.title: Object Lookup
objectLookup.description: Determines whether object lookup should occur. If true, existing object is queried. Otherwise, object in shared state is used for template object.

EmailTemplateNode.name: Email Template Node
EmailTemplateNode.help: Sends an email to the user.

identityAttribute.description: The attribute used to identify the the object in IDM.

emailAttribute.description: The IDM email field to send the email to.

IdentifyExistingUserNode.name: Identify Existing User
IdentifyExistingUserNode.help: Verifies a user with the specified identity attribute exists.

identifier.title: Identifier
identifier.description: The IDM attribute used to save existing value in sharedState for log in purposes.

identityAttribute.title: Identity Attribute
identityAttribute.description: The attribute used to retrieve an existing user.

IncrementLoginCountNode.name: Increment Login Count
IncrementLoginCountNode.help: Increments the successful login count property of a user.

identityAttribute.title: Identity Attribute
identityAttribute.description: The attribute of the IDM object to use retrieve the object.

InnerTreeEvaluatorNode.name: Inner Tree Evaluator
InnerTreeEvaluatorNode.help: Allows the nesting and evaluation of authentication trees as children within a parent tree.

displayErrorOutcome.title: Error Outcome
displayErrorOutcome.description: Displays the outcome to return if an exception occurs during the evaluation of the inner tree. If disabled, the exception is propagated to the parent tree instead.

tree.title: Tree Name
tree.description: The name of the tree that will be evaluated.

KbaCreateNode.name: KBA Definition
KbaCreateNode.help: Collects KBA question and answer definitions from user and saves the provided input.

allowUserDefinedQuestions.title: Allow User-Defined Questions
allowUserDefinedQuestions.description: When enabled, users are able to create their own KBA questions.

message.title: Purpose Message
message.description: Localised message describing the purpose of the data requested from the user.

KbaDecisionNode.name: KBA Decision
KbaDecisionNode.help: Checks if the number of KBA questions defined for the user meets the minimum required by the system.

identityAttribute.title: Identity Attribute
identityAttribute.description: The attribute of the IDM object to use retrieve the object.

KbaVerifyNode.name: KBA Verification
KbaVerifyNode.help: Presents KBA questions to the user, collects answers to those questions, and verifies the input against the user's stored answers.

kbaInfoAttribute.title: KBA Attribute
kbaInfoAttribute.description: The attribute in the user object in IDM where KBA questions and answers are stored.

identityAttribute.title: Identity Attribute
identityAttribute.description: The IDM attribute used to identify the object in a search, e.g. userName.

LoginCountDecisionNode.name: Login Count Decision
LoginCountDecisionNode.help: Compares the user's login count property against a specified condition.

interval.title: Interval
interval.description: The interval type for which the decision should occur. Valid types are 'every' and 'at'.

identityAttribute.title: Identity Attribute
identityAttribute.description: The attribute of the IDM object to use retrieve the object.

amount.title: Amount
amount.description: The amount (count) of logins for the interval.

ModifyAuthLevelNode.name: Modify Auth Level
ModifyAuthLevelNode.help: Increases or decreases the current authentication level value.

authLevelIncrement.title: Value To Add
authLevelIncrement.description: Value which is added to the authentication level.Value may be negative to decrease the authentication level.

PageNode.name: Page Node
PageNode.help: Combines multiple nodes that request input into a single page for display to the user.

pageHeader.title: Page Header
pageHeader.description: Localisation overrides - as key fill shortcut for language (first will be used as default if not empty or "Default header" if empty), value is header for language defined by key

pageDescription.title: Page Description
pageDescription.description: Localisation overrides - as key fill shortcut for language (first will be used as default if not empty or "Default description" if empty), value is description for language defined by key

objectType.description: The name of the ICF object type for the object being authenticated.

PatchObjectNode.help: Patches an object with the attributes within shared state.

identityAttribute.description: The attribute used to identify the the object in IDM.

ignoredFields.description: Fields from sharedState that should be ignored as part of patch. If empty, all fields are attempted as part of the patch.

patchAsObject.description: Whether the patch should be done as object or client. Defaults to false, which represents the oauth client.

identityResource.description: The identity resource in IDM that this node will patch. This is used to aid node input requirement declaration.  Must match identity resource of the current tree.

ProfileCompletenessDecisionNode.help: Checks if the user's profile contains a specified percentage of non-null, user-viewable, and user-editable fields.

ProvisionDynamicAccountNode.help: Provisions an account following successful authentication by a social identity provider node.

identityAttribute.description: The attribute used to retrieve the object to be tested against the query filter.

queryFilter.description: A query filter tested against an object.

SelectIdPNode.name: Select Identity Provider
SelectIdPNode.help: Prompts the user to choose a social ID provider.

filteredProviders.title: Filter Enabled Providers
filteredProviders.description: Add the names of enabled identity providers to use. If not specified, the node uses all providers enabled in the "Social Identity Provider Service".

passwordAttribute.title: Password attribute
passwordAttribute.description: The attribute in the user object that verifies that user during local authentication. Requires Platform deployment with IDM present.

includeLocalAuthentication.title: Include local authentication
includeLocalAuthentication.description: Whether local authentication will be included as an available identity provider.

identityAttribute.title: Identity Attribute
identityAttribute.description: The attribute used to retrieve an existing user. Required to support the offer of only existing providers. Requires Platform deployment with IDM present.

offerOnlyExisting.title: Offer only existing providers
offerOnlyExisting.description: Choices offered should be limited to those already associated with a user object. Requires Platform deployment with IDM present.

SessionDataNode.name: Get Session Data
SessionDataNode.help: Retrieves the value of a specified key from a user's session data.

SetPersistentCookieNode.name: Set Persistent Cookie
SetPersistentCookieNode.help: Creates a persistent cookie named after the value specified in the Persistent cookie name property.

persistentCookieName.title: Persistent Cookie Name
persistentCookieName.description: Sets the name of the persistent cookie.

hmacSigningKey.title: HMAC Signing Key
hmacSigningKey.description: Base64-encoded 256-bit key to use for HMAC signing of the cookie. This property is deprecated. Use the HMAC Signing Key Secret Label Identifier instead. The signing key is  ignored if you set an HMAC Signing Key Secret Label Identifier.

sameSite.title: Persistent Cookie SameSite attribute
sameSite.description:
Sets the same site attribute of the persistent cookie. This value controls when a browser sends the cookie in cross-site requests. Possible values include:
* `STRICT`: The cookie is only sent when navigating within the same site. It's not sent on any cross-site requests, even for top-level navigations.
* `LAX`: The cookie is sent on same-site requests and top-level navigations. It's not sent on subresource requests (like images or scripts loaded from a third-party site).
* `NONE`: There are no restrictions on the cookie domain.

idleTimeout.title: Idle Timeout
idleTimeout.description: The maximum idle time between requests before the cookie is invalidated, in hours.

maxLife.title: Max Life
maxLife.description: The maximum length of time the persistent cookie is valid for, in hours.

hmacSigningKeySecretLabelIdentifier.title: HMAC Signing Key Secret Label Identifier
hmacSigningKeySecretLabelIdentifier.description:
Identifier used to create a secret label for mapping to a secret in a secret store.
AM uses this identifier to create a specific secret label for this node. The secret label takes the form am.authentication.nodes.persistentcookie.{{identifier}}.signing where {{identifier}} is the value of HMAC Signing Key Secret Label Identifier. The identifier can only contain characters {{a-z}} {{A-Z}} {{0-9}} {{.}} and cannot start or end with {{.}}. If you set an HMAC Signing Key Secret Label Identifier and AM finds a matching secret in a secret store, the HMAC Signing Key is ignored.

useSecureCookie.title: Use Secure Cookie
useSecureCookie.description: Sets the persistent cookie as "Secure". This will be ignored and set to true if SameSite is set to "NONE".
